html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 命馆臣録存 - Formal command to scholars in the imperial library to record and preserve something.
  • 杨维桢正统辨谕 - Refers to Yang Weizhen's treatise on the legitimacy of the Yuan dynasty's succession from the Song dynasty.
  • 大防 - Term used in classical Chinese to denote a major principle or argument.
  • 排斥辽金 - Means 'exclude the Liao and Jin,' indicating Yang Weizhen's stance against recognizing these dynasties as legitimate successors.
  • 陶宗仪辍耕录 - Title of a historical record compiled by Tao Zongyi, which includes Yang Weizhen's work.
  • 四库全书 - Vast collection of books commissioned by the Qing dynasty, known as the 'Complete Library in Four Branches.'
  • 纰缪 - Means 'inaccurate' or 'erroneous,' used to criticize Yang Weizhen's argument.
  • 隋先代周继乃平陈 - Historical reference to the Sui dynasty's succession from the Zhou dynasty after conquering the Chen dynasty.
  • 唐宋诸儒谓隋承陈不承周 - Refers to the view held by scholars of the Tang and Song dynasties that the Sui dynasty should be seen as succeeding the Chen dynasty rather than the Zhou dynasty.
  • 此语似是而非 - Means 'this statement seems correct but is actually incorrect,' indicating a nuanced criticism of the argument.
  • 春秋大一统之义 - Refers to the Confucian principle of 'great unity' (da yitong), emphasizing the importance of a unified political order.
  • 尊王黜霸 - Means 'uphold the king and reject the hegemon,' a Confucian ideal advocating for a centralized, morally just government.
  • 立万世之纲常 - Means 'establish the norms and principles for all time,' emphasizing the enduring nature of moral and political standards.
  • 使名正言顺 - Means 'to make names and words right and proper,' suggesting the importance of correct terminology and rhetoric.
  • 出于天命人心之正 - Means 'arising from the will of heaven and the hearts of people,' emphasizing the divine and popular legitimacy of the political order.
  • 紫阳纲目义在正统 - Refers to the 'Zizhi Tongjian' (Comprehensive Mirror to Aid in Government) by Sima Guang, which emphasizes the importance of a legitimate succession.
  • 始皇之廿六年 - Refers to the 26th year of Qin Shi Huang's reign, marking the beginning of the Qin dynasty.
  • 三国不以魏吴之强夺汉统之正 - Means 'the Three Kingdoms did not usurp the legitimate succession of the Han dynasty despite the strength of Wei and Wu,' emphasizing the moral and political legitimacy of the Han dynasty.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 杨维桢 - Prominent scholar and poet of the Yuan dynasty, known for his works on political and historical topics.
  • 宋辽金 - Refers to the Song, Liao, and Jin dynasties, which were contemporaneous and often in conflict.
  • 元 - The Mongol-led Yuan dynasty, which ruled China from 1271 to 1368.
  • 陶宗仪 - Yuan dynasty historian and compiler of the '辍耕录,' a collection of historical anecdotes and records.
  • 四库全书 - Massive compilation of Chinese literature and scholarship commissioned by the Qing dynasty, covering a wide range of subjects.
  • 隋 - The Sui dynasty, which reunified China after the division of the Northern and Southern dynasties.
  • 周 - The Zhou dynasty, which preceded the Qin dynasty and was overthrown by Qin Shi Huang.
  • 陈 - The Chen dynasty, the last of the Southern dynasties, which was conquered by the Sui dynasty.
  • 唐宋诸儒 - Refers to the scholars and philosophers of the Tang and Song dynasties, who were influential in shaping Chinese thought and political theory.
  • 春秋 - The 'Spring and Autumn Annals,' a historical text that is foundational to Confucian thought and emphasizes the importance of moral and political legitimacy.
  • 紫阳纲目 - Refers to the 'Zizhi Tongjian' (Comprehensive Mirror to Aid in Government) by Sima Guang, a monumental historical text that emphasizes the importance of a legitimate succession.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 三史正统辨 - A term referring to a discussion or argument about the legitimacy of the dynastic succession in Chinese history.
  • 凡二千六百余言 - A phrase indicating the length of the text, meaning 'totaling over two thousand six hundred words.'
  • 义本春秋法宗纲目 - A phrase suggesting that the argument is based on the principles of the *Spring and Autumn Annals* and follows the methods of the *Comprehensive Mirror to Aid in Government*.
  • 元继南宋为正统而不及辽金 - A statement indicating that the author argues the Yuan Dynasty should be considered the legitimate successor to the Southern Song Dynasty, excluding the Liao and Jin Dynasties.
  • 持论颇正不得谓之纰缪 - A phrase meaning that the argument is quite correct and cannot be considered mistaken or erroneous.
  • 维桢身为元臣入明虽不仕而应明太祖之召且上铙歌鼓吹曲颂美新朝非刺故国 - A complex sentence indicating that the author, as a former official of the Yuan Dynasty, did not serve in the Ming Dynasty but was invited by the founding emperor of the Ming Dynasty and composed music and poetry to praise the new dynasty while not criticizing the old one.
  • 几于剧秦美新 - A phrase meaning that the author's actions are almost as if he were praising the new dynasty like Jia Yi did during the Han Dynasty, who wrote *Lament for the South* to praise the Han and criticize the Qin.
  • 向屡于诗文中斥之 - A phrase indicating that the author has often criticized the individual in previous writings.
  • 其进退无据较之钱谦益托言不忘故君者鄙倍尤甚 - A phrase meaning that the author's lack of consistency in his actions is even more despicable than that of Qian Qianyi, who claimed to remember his former ruler.
  • 夫正统者继前统受新命也 - A phrase defining the concept of a legitimate dynasty as one that inherits the previous dynasty and receives a new mandate.
  • 东晋以后宋齐梁陈虽江左偏安而所承者晋之正统 - A phrase indicating that after the Eastern Jin Dynasty, the Southern Dynasties (Song, Qi, Liang, and Chen) were considered the legitimate successors to the Eastern Jin, despite being confined to the southern part of China.
  • 其所承之统正也 - A phrase emphasizing that the legitimacy of the Southern Dynasties' rule was based on their inheritance of the Eastern Jin's mandate.
  • 至隋则平陈以后混一区宇始得为大一统 - A phrase indicating that the Sui Dynasty, after conquering the Chen Dynasty, unified all of China and thus achieved a great unification.
  • 即唐之末季藩镇扰乱自朱温以讫郭威等或起自冦窃或身为叛臣五十余年之间更易数姓甚且称臣称侄于契丹然中国统绪相承宋以前亦不得不以正统属之梁唐晋汉周也 - A complex sentence indicating that during the late Tang Dynasty, the regional military governors (fanzhen) caused chaos, and from Zhu Wen to Guo Wei, some rose from banditry and others were traitors. Over fifty years, several dynasties succeeded each other, and even some paid homage to the Khitan (Liao Dynasty). Despite this, the legitimate succession before the Song Dynasty had to be attributed to the Later Liang, Later Tang, Later Jin, Later Han, and Later Zhou.
  • 至于宋南渡后偏处临安其时辽金元相继起于北邉奄有河北宋虽称侄于金而其所承者究仍北宋之正统辽金不得攘而有之也 - A phrase indicating that after the Southern Song Dynasty retreated south to Lin'an (Hangzhou), the Liao, Jin, and Yuan Dynasties rose in the north. Although the Song Dynasty paid homage to the Jin Dynasty, it still inherited the legitimate mandate of the Northern Song Dynasty, and the Liao and Jin could not claim it.
  • 至元世祖平宋始有宋统当絶我统当续之语则统绪之正元世祖已知之稔矣我 - A phrase indicating that when Kublai Khan (Yuan Shizu) conquered the Song Dynasty, he recognized that the Song Dynasty's mandate had ended and the Yuan Dynasty's mandate had begun, and he was well aware of the legitimacy of the Yuan Dynasty's succession.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 三史 - Refers to the *Book of Han*, *Book of the Later Han*, and *Records of the Three Kingdoms*, which are part of the Twenty-Four Histories.
  • 春秋 - Refers to the *Spring and Autumn Annals*, a Confucian classic that records the history of the State of Lu.
  • 纲目 - Refers to the *Comprehensive Mirror to Aid in Government* (*资治通鉴*), a monumental historical work compiled by Sima Guang.
  • 剧秦美新 - Refers to the work *Lament for the South* (*过秦论*) by Jia Yi, which praised the Han Dynasty and criticized the Qin Dynasty.
  • 钱谦益 - A prominent scholar and politician of the late Ming and early Qing Dynasties, known for his loyalty to the Ming Dynasty and his later collaboration with the Qing Dynasty.
  • 正统 - The concept of a legitimate dynasty, which is a central theme in Chinese political and historical discourse.
  • 东晋以后宋齐梁陈 - Refers to the Southern Dynasties, a series of short-lived dynasties that ruled the southern part of China after the fall of the Eastern Jin Dynasty.
  • 隋 - The Sui Dynasty, which unified China after the fall of the Northern and Southern Dynasties.
  • 唐之末季藩镇扰乱 - Refers to the period of the late Tang Dynasty, when regional military governors (fanzhen) caused significant political and military instability.
  • 朱温以讫郭威等 - Refers to key figures during the late Tang Dynasty and the Five Dynasties period, including Zhu Wen, the founder of the Later Liang Dynasty, and Guo Wei, the founder of the Later Zhou Dynasty.
  • 宋南渡后偏处临安 - Refers to the Southern Song Dynasty, which retreated south to Lin'an (modern-day Hangzhou) after the fall of the Northern Song Dynasty.
  • 辽金元 - Refers to the Liao, Jin, and Yuan Dynasties, which ruled the northern part of China during the same period as the Southern Song Dynasty.
  • 元世祖 - Refers to Kublai Khan, the founder of the Yuan Dynasty, who conquered the Southern Song Dynasty and established the Yuan Dynasty.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 大公至正 - Means 'greatly impartial and righteous,' describing a standard that is both fair and morally correct.
  • 阳九之运 - Refers to a period of misfortune or calamity, often used in classical Chinese to describe a time of chaos or decline.
  • 天弗与人弗归 - Means 'heaven does not favor and people do not return,' indicating a loss of divine and human support.
  • 自覆其宗社 - Means 'self-destructed their ancestral temple,' referring to the Ming dynasty's downfall.
  • 辍耕录中所载杨维桢之正统辨 - Refers to the *Chuogeng Lu* (Record of Ceasing Plowing) by Yang Weizhen, which contains his argument about the legitimacy of the ruling dynasty.
  • 馆臣之删杨维桢正统辨者 - Refers to the scholars in the imperial library who edited out Yang Weizhen's argument about the legitimacy of the ruling dynasty.
  • 金为满洲欲令承辽之统 - Means 'to make Jin (Jurchen) the predecessor of the Manchus and have them inherit the legacy of the Liao dynasty.'
  • 辽金皆自起北方本无所承继 - Means 'both Liao and Jin originated in the north and had no legitimate succession.'
  • 特谕 - Means 'special edict,' indicating a formal imperial decree.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 皇祖 - Refers to the emperor's ancestors, specifically the early Qing emperors.
  • 通鉴 - Refers to the *Zizhi Tongjian* (Comprehensive Mirror for Aid in Government), a monumental historical text compiled during the Song dynasty.
  • 通鉴辑览 - Refers to the *Tongjian Jiluan* (Comprehensive Mirror Compilation), a later compilation based on the *Zizhi Tongjian*.
  • 春秋大义 - Refers to the principles of the *Spring and Autumn Annals*, a Confucian classic that emphasizes moral and political principles.
  • 纲目大法 - Refers to the *Gangmu* (Outline and Categories), a method of organizing historical events and moral lessons.
  • 福王 - Refers to the last emperor of the Southern Ming dynasty, Zhu Yousong, who ruled briefly after the fall of the Ming capital.
  • 南宋 - Refers to the Southern Song dynasty, which ruled southern China after the fall of the Northern Song.
  • 杨维桢 - A famous Ming dynasty scholar and poet known for his political writings and commentary on historical events.
  • 正统辨 - Refers to debates about the legitimacy of ruling dynasties, particularly the transition from the Yuan to the Ming.

Proper Nouns

  • 皇祖 - The emperor's ancestors.
  • 通鉴 - The *Zizhi Tongjian* (Comprehensive Mirror for Aid in Government).
  • 通鉴辑览 - The *Tongjian Jiluan* (Comprehensive Mirror Compilation).
  • 春秋大义 - The principles of the *Spring and Autumn Annals*.
  • 纲目大法 - The *Gangmu* (Outline and Categories).
  • 福王 - Zhu Yousong, the last emperor of the Southern Ming.
  • 南宋 - The Southern Song dynasty.
  • 杨维桢 - A famous Ming dynasty scholar and poet.
  • 正统辨 - Debates about the legitimacy of ruling dynasties.
  • 金 - The Jurchen (ancestors of the Manchus).
  • 辽 - The Liao dynasty.
  • 宋 - The Song dynasty.
  • 元 - The Yuan dynasty.
  • 特谕 - A special imperial edict.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 乾隆辛丑孟春 - In the first month of spring in the Xinchou year of the Qianlong era.
  • 欽定四庫全書 - The Complete Library in Four Branches of Literature, officially compiled.
  • 子部十二 - The twelfth section of the Zibu (Subsection) in the classification system.
  • 提要 - Introduction or Preface, often used in bibliographic entries.
  • 陶宗儀 - Tao Zongyi, the author of the text being discussed.
  • 國風尊經 - The Respect for the Classics in the National Style, another work by Tao Zongyi.
  • 至正丙午 - In the Bingwu year of the Zhizheng era, referring to the year 1366 during the late Yuan dynasty.
  • 孫作序 - Sun Zuo wrote the preface, indicating that Sun Zuo wrote an introduction to the book.
  • 集慶軍 - Jiading Army, a military force in the region of present-day Nanjing.
  • 江南游軍 - Southern Jiangnan Army, another name for the same military force.
  • 廣客談 - Expanded Guest Talks, a text that Tao Zongyi is said to have borrowed from.
  • 通本録之 - Recorded it in full, indicating that Tao Zongyi copied the entire text.
  • 攘為己作 - Claimed it as his own, suggesting that Tao Zongyi plagiarized.
  • 有元一代 - Throughout the Yuan dynasty, referring to the entire period of Mongol rule in China.
  • 法令制度 - Laws and institutions, referring to the legal and administrative systems of the Yuan dynasty.
  • 至正東南兵亂之事 - The military upheavals in southeastern China during the Zhizheng era, referring to the political instability in the late Yuan dynasty.
  • 考訂 - Verification or examination, referring to the process of cross-checking historical records.
  • 書畫文藝 - Calligraphy, painting, and literary arts, referring to the cultural achievements of the Yuan dynasty.
  • 足備防證 - Sufficient to provide evidence, indicating that the records are valuable for historical verification.
  • 俚俗戲謔之語 - Vulgar and humorous language, referring to colloquial and playful expressions.
  • 乖著作之體 - Contrary to the style of writing, indicating that the inclusion of such material is inappropriate for a scholarly work.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 乾隆 - The reign title of Emperor Qianlong (1736–1795), the sixth emperor of the Qing dynasty.
  • 辛丑 - The sexagenary cycle for the year 1731, the first year of Qianlong's reign.
  • 欽定四庫全書 - A monumental compilation of Chinese literature commissioned by Emperor Qianlong, completed in 1782.
  • 子部十二 - The twelfth section of the Zibu (Subsection) in the classification system, which includes novels and miscellaneous writings.
  • 提要 - A common term in Chinese bibliographies, used to summarize the content of a book.
  • 陶宗儀 - A Yuan dynasty scholar and writer, best known for his work *辍耕录* (*Records of a Plowman's Rest*).
  • 國風尊經 - Another work by Tao Zongyi, though less well-known than *辍耕录*.
  • 至正丙午 - The year 1366, during the late Yuan dynasty, when Tao Zongyi wrote *辍耕录*.
  • 孫作序 - Sun Zuo, a contemporary of Tao Zongyi, who wrote the preface to *辍耕录*.
  • 集慶軍 - A military force in the region of present-day Nanjing, active during the late Yuan dynasty.
  • 江南游軍 - Another name for the same military force, emphasizing its role in the southern regions.
  • 廣客談 - A text that Lang Ying claims Tao Zongyi copied from.
  • 通本録之 - Indicating that Tao Zongyi recorded the entire text of *廣客談*.
  • 攘為己作 - Suggesting that Tao Zongyi claimed the text as his own, which is a serious accusation of plagiarism.
  • 有元一代 - The entire period of Mongol rule in China (1271–1368).
  • 法令制度 - The legal and administrative systems of the Yuan dynasty, which were influenced by both Mongol and Chinese traditions.
  • 至正東南兵亂之事 - The military upheavals in southeastern China during the late Yuan dynasty, which contributed to the fall of the dynasty.
  • 考訂 - The process of verifying historical records, which was an important scholarly activity in the Yuan dynasty.
  • 書畫文藝 - The cultural achievements of the Yuan dynasty, particularly in calligraphy, painting, and literature.
  • 足備防證 - Indicating that the records in *辍耕录* are valuable for historical verification.
  • 俚俗戲謔之語 - Colloquial and humorous language, which was often included in Yuan dynasty literature but may not be appropriate for scholarly works.
  • 乖著作之體 - Indicating that the inclusion of such material is inappropriate for a scholarly work.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 深病其所载猥防 - Deeply criticize the content as being overly cautious or restrained.
  • 良非苛论 - Indeed not a harsh critique.
  • 首尾赅贯 - From beginning to end, comprehensive and coherent.
  • 要为能留心于掌故 - Essentially, it shows a careful attention to historical anecdotes.
  • 练习旧章 - Familiar with old regulations or customs.
  • 虽瑜不掩瑕 - Even with its merits, it cannot hide its flaws.
  • 论古者所不废 - Not discarded by those who discuss ancient matters.
  • 掇拾缕载 - Picked up and recorded in detail.
  • 寡识 - Lacking in knowledge or insight.
  • 昭公义 - Clarify public righteousness.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 天台陶君九成 - Refers to Tao Zongyi, a scholar from Taishan (天台), who was known as 'Jiu Cheng' (九成).
  • 南村辍耕录 - A historical and literary work by Tao Zongyi, documenting his observations and writings during his time in the countryside.
  • 六经百氏 - Refers to the Six Classics and various schools of thought in Confucianism.
  • 稗官小史 - Refers to minor or unofficial historical records and folklore.
  • 杨维桢 - A Yuan Dynasty scholar and author of *Zhengtong Bian* (正统辨).

Proper Nouns

  • 杨维桢 - Yang Weizhen, a Yuan Dynasty scholar and author of *Zhengtong Bian*.
  • 乾隆四十六年 - The 46th year of the Qianlong Emperor's reign (1781).

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 采摭之博侈 - Refers to the extensive and rich collection of materials.
  • 伤今慨古 - Expresses a sense of lamenting the present and reflecting on the past.
  • 铺张盛美 - Describes the elaboration of grand and beautiful themes.
  • 忠臣孝子之劝 - Refers to the encouragement of loyalty and filial piety.
  • 文章制度 - Refers to the rules and conventions of literary composition.
  • 疑似根据可览而悉 - Suggests that the work provides clear evidence and explanations for its claims.
  • 唐宋以来专门史学之所未让 - Indicates that the work surpasses the specialized historical scholarship of the Tang and Song dynasties.
  • 周室之藏 - Refers to the vast knowledge and resources of the Zhou dynasty.
  • 郯子之对 - Refers to the wisdom and responses of Tán Zǐ, a wise man from the Spring and Autumn period.
  • 环辙 - Refers to the circular paths or tracks, symbolizing the comprehensive nature of the work.
  • 抵掌谈笑 - Describes a casual and informal manner of conversation.
  • 优孟 - Refers to a famous jester from the Warring States period known for his wisdom and wit.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 白帖 - A type of anthology or collection of texts.
  • 洪笔 - Refers to grand and powerful writing.
  • 伤今慨古 - Expresses a sense of lamenting the present and reflecting on the past.
  • 铺张盛美 - Describes the elaboration of grand and beautiful themes.
  • 忠臣孝子之劝 - Refers to the encouragement of loyalty and filial piety.
  • 文章制度 - Refers to the rules and conventions of literary composition.
  • 疑似根据可览而悉 - Suggests that the work provides clear evidence and explanations for its claims.
  • 唐宋以来专门史学之所未让 - Indicates that the work surpasses the specialized historical scholarship of the Tang and Song dynasties.
  • 周室之藏 - Refers to the vast knowledge and resources of the Zhou dynasty.
  • 郯子之对 - Refers to the wisdom and responses of Tán Zǐ, a wise man from the Spring and Autumn period.
  • 环辙 - Refers to the circular paths or tracks, symbolizing the comprehensive nature of the work.
  • 抵掌谈笑 - Describes a casual and informal manner of conversation.
  • 优孟 - Refers to a famous jester from the Warring States period known for his wisdom and wit.

Proper Nouns

  • 九成名宗仪 - The author's name.
  • 至正丙午 - The year 1356, using the sexagenary cycle.
  • 江隂 - A place name, referring to Jiangyin.
  • 孙作大雅 - The name of the person who wrote the preface, Sun Dàyǎ.

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 列聖授受正統 - Formal phrase meaning 'the legitimate succession of the sage emperors,' emphasizing dynastic continuity.
  • 始祖 - Refers to the founding ancestor of a lineage or dynasty.
  • 宋開禧二年丙寅十二月 - Specific date: the year 1206 CE, when Genghis Khan declared himself Khan.
  • 宋寳慶三年丁亥七月己丑 - Specific date: the year 1227 CE, when Genghis Khan died.
  • 太宗英文皇帝 - Posthumous title for Ögedei Khan, meaning 'great emperor, literary emperor.'
  • 睿宗仁聖景襄皇帝 - Posthumous title for Tolui Khan, meaning 'wise ancestor, benevolent and saintly, illustrious and extensive emperor.'
  • 定宗簡平皇帝 - Posthumous title for Guyuk Khan, meaning 'settled emperor, simple and peaceful emperor.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 列聖 - Refers to the emperors of the Mongol empire, emphasizing their legitimacy and divine right.
  • 授受正統 - Concept from Confucian political theory, emphasizing the proper succession of rulers.
  • 起辇谷 - Historical burial site for Mongol emperors, located in modern-day Mongolia.
  • 皇后塔納竒納 - Name 'Töregene Khatun,' who served as regent during the reign of Möngke Khan.
  • 皇后鼐瑪錦氏 - Name 'Naimanjin Khatun,' another consort of Genghis Khan.

Proper Nouns

  • 列聖 - The emperors of the Mongol empire.
  • 始祖 - The founding ancestor of the Mongol lineage.
  • 烈祖神元皇帝 - Yisüngge, the founder of the Mongol empire.
  • 太祖應天啓運聖武皇帝 - Genghis Khan, the founder of the Mongol empire.
  • 鄂諾河 - The Onon River, where Genghis Khan declared himself Khan.
  • 薩里川 - The Sarayu River area, where Genghis Khan died.
  • 起辇谷 - The burial site for Mongol emperors.

[8]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 即位 - Formal expression meaning 'ascended to the throne' or 'became emperor.'
  • 崩于 - Respectful term meaning 'died' or 'passed away,' often used in reference to emperors.
  • 寿 - Refers to the age of the emperor at the time of death.
  • 追谥 - Posthumous title or honorific bestowed on an emperor after their death.
  • 国语曰 - Indicates that the emperor's name is given in the native language (Mongolian in this case).

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 宪宗桓肃皇帝【讳孟克】 - Refers to the second emperor of the Yuan dynasty, who reigned from 1251 to 1259.
  • 世祖圣德神功文武皇帝【讳呼必赉国语曰色辰】 - Refers to Kublai Khan, the founder of the Yuan dynasty, who reigned from 1260 to 1294.
  • 顺宗昭圣衍孝皇帝【讳达尔玛巴拉】 - Refers to Kublai Khan's grandson, who was posthumously given this title.
  • 成宗钦明广孝皇帝【讳特穆尔国语曰谔勒哲图】 - Refers to Temür Khan, Kublai Khan's grandson and the third emperor of the Yuan dynasty, who reigned from 1294 to 1307.
  • 武宗仁惠宣孝皇帝【讳哈尚国语曰库鲁克】 - Refers to Yesün Temür, the fourth emperor of the Yuan dynasty, who reigned from 1308 to 1311.
  • 起辇谷 - A place where the Yuan emperors were buried, located in present-day Mongolia.
  • 建元中统 - Refers to the era name 'Zhongtong' (1260–1264) used during Kublai Khan's reign.
  • 改至元三十二为元贞 - Refers to the change of era name from 'Zhongtong' to 'Yuanzhen' (1295–1297) during Temür Khan's reign.
  • 诏改大徳十二为至大 - Refers to the change of era name from 'Dade' (1299–1307) to 'Zhida' (1308–1311) during Yesün Temür's reign.

Proper Nouns

  • 宪宗桓肃皇帝 - The second emperor of the Yuan dynasty.
  • 世祖圣德神功文武皇帝 - Kublai Khan, the founder of the Yuan dynasty.
  • 顺宗昭圣衍孝皇帝 - Kublai Khan's grandson, posthumously given this title.
  • 成宗钦明广孝皇帝 - Temür Khan, Kublai Khan's grandson and the third emperor of the Yuan dynasty.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 仁宗聖文欽孝皇帝 - A title combining '仁宗' (Emperor Renzong), '聖文' (Holy Literature), '欽孝' (Revered Filial Piety), and '皇帝' (Emperor). This is a formal title indicating the emperor's virtues and status.
  • 至大四年辛亥三月十八日庚寅即位于大明殿 - Describes the specific date and location of the emperor's ascension to the throne. '至大四年' refers to the fourth year of the Zhida era, '辛亥' is the stem-branch calendar system, '三月十八日庚寅' specifies the day, and '大明殿' is the name of the palace.
  • 九月壬子诏改至大五为皇庆 - Indicates a change in the era name from 'Zhida' to 'Huangqing' in the fifth year of the Zhida era.
  • 延祐七年庚申正月二十一日辛丑崩于光天宫 - Describes the death of Emperor Renzong on the 21st day of the first month of the seventh year of the Yanyou era, in the 'Guangtian Palace'.
  • 在位九年夀三十六 - Summarizes the emperor's reign duration and age at death.

  • 金人谋欲厌胜之 - Means 'the Jin people planned to suppress or counteract it.'

  • 金人乃大发卒凿掘辇运 - Means 'the Jin people then mobilized troops to excavate and transport the mountain.'

  • 遂赐今名云 - Means 'and thus was given its current name.'

  • 专掌宫禁工役者 - Means 'it was responsible for overseeing the construction and maintenance of the imperial city.'
  • 大军渡河 - Means 'the main army crossed the river.'
  • 世皇取江南 - Means 'the World Emperor (Kublai Khan) conquered the southern territories.'
  • 大军次黄河苦乏舟楫 - Means 'the main army camped by the Yellow River, lacking boats and rafts.'
  • 夜梦一老曰 - Means 'one night, he dreamed of an old man who said.'
  • 陛下欲渡河当随我来 - Means 'If Your Majesty wishes to cross the river, follow me.'
  • 引至一所指曰此即是已 - Means 'he led him to a place and said, 'This is it.''
  • 帝遂以物标识之 - Means 'the emperor then marked the place with a sign.'
  • 及觉歴歴可记 - Means 'upon waking, he could clearly remember it.'
  • 明日循行河浒寻梦中所见处 - Means 'the next day, he followed the riverbank and found the place he had seen in his dream.'
  • 果是方惊顾间忽有人进曰此间水浅可渡 - Means 'it was indeed the place, and just as he was surprised, someone said, 'The water here is shallow enough to cross.''
  • 时帝征梦中语因谓曰汝能先涉否 - Means 'At that time, the emperor recalled his dream and asked, 'Can you cross first?'
  • 其人乃行大军自后从之无一不济 - Means 'the person then crossed, and the entire army followed, and none failed to cross.'
  • 帝欲重旌其功对曰富与贵悉非所愿但得自在足矣 - Means 'The emperor wanted to reward him greatly, but the person replied, 'Wealth and status are not my desires; I am content with freedom.'
  • 遂封为达尔罕与五品印拨三百戸以食之 - Means 'He was then enfeoffed as Dalaihan, given a fifth-rank seal, and granted three hundred households as his fief.'
  • 今其子孙尚有存者 - Means 'Today, his descendants still exist.'
  • 此事杨元诚太史瑀所云 - Means 'This is what Yang Yuancheng, the historian Yu, said.'

  • 荐之于世祖 - Recommended to Emperor Shizu, referring to Kublai Khan.
  • 征不至 - Refused the summons, indicating refusal to accept an imperial appointment.

  • 寻复得告还山 - Later received permission to return to the mountains, indicating retirement.

  • 文贞王额琳特穆尔 - A title and name, possibly referring to a historical figure.

  • 吠数声而去 - Barked a few times and left, a metaphor for a minimal response.
  • 却有甚好听处 - But it’s quite amusing to listen to, suggesting irony or humor in the situation.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 防即卷字 - Explanation: '防' is the same as '卷' (scroll), indicating that '防' is a variant or alternative term for '卷.'
  • 真诰中谓一卷为一防 - Explanation: From the *Zhengao* (True Scriptures), one scroll is referred to as one '防,' suggesting a specific usage in a religious or esoteric text.
  • 或以为吊字及篇字者皆非 - Explanation: Some mistakenly equate '防' with '吊' (mourning) or '篇' (chapter), but these interpretations are incorrect.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 真诰 - Explanation: A text from the *Zhengao* (True Scriptures), a collection of Taoist scriptures.
  • 吊字及篇字 - Explanation: These terms are mentioned as incorrect interpretations of '防,' highlighting the importance of accurate terminology in classical Chinese texts.
  • 辍耕録 - Explanation: A historical and literary work compiled by Tao Zongyi during the Yuan dynasty, known for its detailed records and observations.
  • 钦定四库全书 - Explanation: A monumental project initiated by Emperor Qianlong to compile and preserve Chinese literature and scholarship.

Proper Nouns

  • 吊 - Explanation: A term for mourning or lamentation.
  • 篇 - Explanation: A term for chapter or section.

Idiomatic Expressions

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 春秋之王正月公羊谓大一统之书 - Refers to the *Gongyang Commentary* on the *Spring and Autumn Annals*, which emphasizes the concept of 'Great Unity.'
  • 纲目之绍春秋 - Refers to the *Zizhi Tongjian* or similar historical texts that continue the tradition of the *Spring and Autumn Annals*.
  • 始皇二十六年而继周统 - Refers to Qin Shi Huang's 26th year of reign, marking the end of the Zhou dynasty and the beginning of the Qin dynasty.
  • 当世祖命巴延平江南之时式应宋祖命曹彬下江南之岁 - Refers to the Yuan dynasty's conquest of southern China and its alignment with the Song dynasty's historical precedent.
  • 确定统宗有继宋不继辽之禅 - Refers to the Yuan dynasty's legitimacy, which follows the Song dynasty rather than the Liao dynasty.
  • 冒干天听深惧氷兢 - Refers to the author's humility and fear of offending the emperor.
  • 下情无任瞻天望阙激切屏营之至 - Refers to the author's deep respect and anxiety in addressing the emperor.
  • 辨曰正统之说何自而起乎 - Refers to the philosophical debate about the origins of the concept of legitimate succession.
  • 起于夏后国汤武革世皆出于天命人心之公也 - Refers to the historical legitimacy of the Shang and Zhou dynasties, which arose from the public will of heaven and humanity.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 三代而下 - Refers to the period after the Three Dynasties (Xia, Shang, Zhou), indicating a historical context.
  • 厯数之相仍者 - Literally 'the succession of the heavenly mandate,' referring to the legitimacy of ruling dynasties.
  • 可以妄归于人乎 - A rhetorical question meaning 'can it be arbitrarily attributed to anyone?'
  • 正统之义 - Refers to the concept of legitimate succession or the rightful line of rulers.
  • 立于圣人之经 - Means 'established in the teachings of sages,' referring to the Confucian classics.
  • 扶万世之纲常 - Means 'uphold the moral principles for all generations.'
  • 春秋是也 - Refers to the *Spring and Autumn Annals* (*Chunqiu*), a historical text attributed to Confucius.
  • 万代之史宗 - Means 'the model of history for all generations.'
  • 首书王正于鲁史之元年者 - Refers to the opening of the *Spring and Autumn Annals* with the title 'King,' emphasizing the legitimacy of the Zhou dynasty.
  • 大一统也 - Means 'the great unification,' referring to the political unity of China.
  • 五伯之权 - Refers to the Five Hegemons, powerful rulers who dominated the Zhou period.
  • 春秋必黜之 - Means 'the *Spring and Autumn Annals* must denounce them,' indicating their rejection of these rulers.
  • 奸此统也 - Means 'they undermine this unification.'
  • 吴楚之号 - Refers to the titles of the states of Wu and Chu.
  • 窃于王也 - Means 'they usurped the title of king.'
  • 春秋必外之 - Means 'the *Spring and Autumn Annals* must exclude them,' indicating their rejection of these states.
  • 僭此统也 - Means 'they usurp this unification.'
  • 尚矣 - An interjection meaning 'indeed' or 'truly.'

  • 春秋之义也 - Means 'in accordance with the principles of the *Spring and Autumn Annals*.'
  • 彼志三国降昭烈以侪吴魏 - Refers to the *Records of the Three Kingdoms* (San Guo Zhi) by Chen Shou, which ranks Liu Bei (Emperor of Shu) as equal to Sun Quan (Emperor of Wu) and Cao Pi (Emperor of Wei).
  • 使汉嗣之正下与汉贼并称 - Means 'making Liu Bei's legitimate succession to the throne of Han equal to that of Cao Pi, who is considered a usurper.'
  • 此春秋之罪人矣 - Means 'this is a sin against the principles of the *Spring and Autumn Annals*.'
  • 复有作元经自谓法春秋者 - Refers to later works that claimed to follow the principles of the *Spring and Autumn Annals*.
  • 而又帝北魏黜江左 - Means 'they also elevated the Northern Wei dynasty and denigrated the Southern dynasties.'
  • 其失与志三国者等耳 - Means 'their mistakes are the same as those of the *Records of the Three Kingdoms*.'
  • 以致尊昭烈续江左两魏之名不正而言不顺者大 - Means 'thus, the elevation of Liu Bei and the Southern dynasties, as well as the Northern Wei, is unjustified and goes against the principles of the *Spring and Autumn Annals*.'

  • 在正统 - Means 'in terms of the legitimate succession.'

  • 又所以法春秋之大一统也 - Means 'and thus follows the *Spring and Autumn Annals*' principle of great unification.'

  • 自我世祖皇帝立国史院尝命承防百一王公修辽金二史矣宋亡又命词臣通修三史矣 - States that the founding emperor established the National History Bureau and appointed Wang Gong to compile the histories of Liao and Jin, and after the Song dynasty's fall, he also commissioned scholars to compile the three histories together.

  • 夫其议未决者又岂不以宋渡于南之后拘于辽金之抗于北乎 - Means 'And isn't it because after the Song dynasty moved south, they were constrained by the resistance of the Liao and Jin in the north?'

  • 八部之雄 - Refers to the leaders of the eight tribes or clans, likely a reference to a historical or mythological context.

  • 迨耶律光而其埶浸盛 - Until the time of Yelü Guang (a name), his power gradually increased, suggesting a period of growing influence.
  • 契丹之号立于梁贞明之初 - The title of Khitan was established at the beginning of Liang's Zhenming era, referring to the founding of the Khitan state.
  • 大辽之号复改于汉天福之日 - The title of Da Liao was changed again on the day of Han's Tianfu era, indicating a change in the state's name or title.
  • 自按巴坚讫于天祚凡七主歴二百一十有五年 - From Anba Jian to Tianzuo (a name), there were seven rulers over 215 years, summarizing the reigns of the Khitan rulers.
  • 辽固唐之边夷也 - Liao was originally a border barbarian of Tang, indicating the Khitan's status as a frontier people.
  • 乘唐之衰草窃而起 - Taking advantage of the decline of Tang, they rose up, suggesting the Khitan's rise to power during the Tang Dynasty's decline.
  • 石晋氏通之且割幽燕以与之 - The Shijie (a name) communicated with them and ceded Youyan (a region) to them, indicating a treaty or agreement.
  • 遂得窥衅中夏而石晋氏不得不亡矣 - Thus they gained the opportunity to invade Central China, and the Shijie could not help but perish, suggesting the Khitan's conquest of Central China.
  • 而议者以辽乘晋统吾不知其何统也 - And the debaters say that Liao succeeded the Shijie, but I do not know what kind of succession it is, indicating a debate about the legitimacy of the Khitan's rule.
  • 再考金之有国矣始于完顔氏实又臣属于契丹者也 - Again, examining the founding of Jin, it began with the Wanyan clan, who were actually subjects of the Khitan, suggesting the Jin Dynasty's origins.
  • 至阿固达苟逃性命于道宗之世遂敢萌人臣之将而簒有其国僭称国号于宋重和之元 - Until Aguda (a name) escaped with his life during the reign of Daozong (a name), he then dared to become a rebel and usurp the country, claiming a false title in the first year of Song's Zhonghe era, indicating the Jin Dynasty's rise to power.
  • 相传九主凡歴一百一十有七年 - Passed down through nine rulers over 117 years, summarizing the reigns of the Jin rulers.
  • 而议者又以金之平辽尅宋帝有中原而谓接辽宋之统吾又不知其何统也 - And the debaters say that Jin succeeded the Liao and conquered Song, claiming the Central Plains, but I do not know what kind of succession it is, indicating a debate about the legitimacy of the Jin's rule.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 世为君长 - Means 'have always been rulers', referring to the long-standing rule of the Wanyan clan.
  • 保其肅慎 - Literally 'preserve their Sushen', referring to the ancient tribe from which the Wanyan clan descended.
  • 太祖时 - Refers to the time of the founding emperor (太祖) of the Jin dynasty.
  • 南北为敌国 - Means 'were enemies to the north and south', indicating the geopolitical situation.

  • 卑辞以告和 - Means 'submitted humble words to seek peace', describing the diplomatic gesture.
  • 结为兄弟 - Means 'formed a brotherly alliance', referring to a formal agreement.
  • 晩年且 - Means 'by the end of their reigns', indicating a later stage.
  • 为翁而宋为孙矣 - Means 'the Liao became the grandfather and the Song became the grandson', a metaphor for the shift in power dynamics.
  • 其说之曲而陋也 - Means 'this argument is biased and shallow', criticizing the previous reasoning.
  • 兴于汉唐之前乎 - Means 'rose before the Han and Tang dynasties', indicating the chronological precedence of the Xiongnu and Tujue tribes.
  • 与之通和矣 - Means 'and they made peace with them', referring to the diplomatic relations.
  • 角立而不相统摄者也 - Means 'stood as rivals without subjugating each other', describing the competitive but equal status.
  • 秉史笔者 - Means 'those who hold the pen of history', referring to historians.
  • 以汉唐为正统 - Means 'and treated the Han and Tang as the legitimate dynasties', emphasizing the historical perspective.
  • 以吴魏为分系 - Means 'treated Wu and Wei as separate branches', indicating the historical categorization.
  • 以蜀汉为正统 - Means 'and treated Shu Han as the legitimate dynasty', emphasizing the historical perspective.
  • 何也 - Means 'why is this so?', a rhetorical question.
  • 天理人心之公 - Means 'the public morality of heaven and human nature', referring to universal ethical principles.
  • 阅万世而不可泯者也 - Means 'which cannot be obliterated through the ages', emphasizing the enduring nature of these principles.
  • 簒逆 - Means 'usurpation and rebellion', referring to illegitimate rule.
  • 不当合为五代史 - Means 'should not be included in the Five Dynasties history', indicating the historical exclusion.
  • 其说似矣 - Means 'this argument seems plausible', acknowledging the surface validity.
  • 吾又不知 - Means 'I do not know', expressing uncertainty.
  • 朱晃之簒克用氏父子 - Means 'Zhu Huang's usurpation of the Ke Yung family', referring to the historical event.
  • 背唐兄弟之约 - Means 'betrayed the brotherly alliance with the Tang', indicating the breach of trust.
  • 称臣于梁 - Means 'submitted as vassals to the Later Liang', describing the diplomatic submission.
  • 非逆党乎 - Means 'are they not rebels?', a rhetorical question.
  • 春秋诛逆重诛其党 - Means 'the *Spring and Autumn Annals* severely punished rebels and their accomplices', referring to the historical text.
  • 契丹 - The name of the Liao dynasty.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 氏之诛为何如哉 - Rhetorical question meaning 'What kind of punishment is this?' or 'How severe is this punishment?'
  • 不受其命而簒唐 - Means 'did not accept the mandate and usurped the Tang.' Describes the act of seizing power without legitimate authority.
  • 谓之承晋可乎 - Rhetorical question meaning 'Can it be called inheriting the Jin?' Challenges the legitimacy of the claim to power.
  • 谓之统可乎 - Rhetorical question meaning 'Can it be called a legitimate succession?' Further questions the legitimacy of the claim.
  • 五代之统论之 - Means 'to discuss the legitimacy of the Five Dynasties.' Refers to the period of Chinese history from 907 to 960 AD, characterized by political instability and frequent dynastic changes.
  • 宋统继唐不优于继周继汉乎 - Rhetorical question meaning 'Is the Song's succession to the Tang better than its succession to the Zhou or Han?' Questions the relative legitimacy of the Song's claim to power.
  • 千载厯数之统不必以承先朝续亡主为正 - Means 'The legitimate succession over thousands of years does not necessarily have to be based on inheriting the previous dynasty or continuing the rule of a fallen emperor.' Challenges the traditional notion of dynastic succession.
  • 宋兴不必以膺周之禅接汉接唐之闰为统也 - Means 'The rise of the Song Dynasty does not have to be based on accepting the abdication of the Zhou, connecting to the Han, or the Tang as an intercalary period.' Further questions the legitimacy of the Song's claim.
  • 欧阳子不定五代为南史为宋膺周禅之张本者皆非矣 - Means 'Ouyang Xiu's determination of the Five Dynasties as the Southern History and the Song accepting the abdication of the Zhou as a precedent is incorrect.' Critiques Ouyang Xiu's historical interpretation.
  • 唐明宗之祝天也 - Means 'When Emperor Mingzong of Tang prayed to heaven.' Refers to a specific historical event.
  • 自以夷虏不任社稷生灵之主 - Means 'He considered himself an uncivilized barbarian and not fit to be the ruler of the state and its people.' Reflects the emperor's self-perception.
  • 愿天早 - Incomplete phrase, likely meaning 'I wish the heavens would act quickly' or 'I hope the heavens will intervene soon.' Expresses a desire for divine intervention.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 生聖人以主生靈 - Classical phrase meaning 'to give birth to a sage to rule the people.'
  • 天人交感 - Refers to the interaction between heaven and humanity, often used to describe divine intervention or cosmic harmony.
  • 君主中國 - Means 'to rule all of China,' emphasizing the legitimacy and universality of the ruler.
  • 非欺孤弱寡之所致也 - A negated causal phrase meaning 'it is not due to the weakness or isolation of the ruler.'
  • 朱氏纲目 - Refers to the 'Zhu Shi Gang Mu' (朱子纲目), a historical text by Zhu Xi, which provides a detailed chronology of Chinese history.
  • 五代之年 - Refers to the Five Dynasties period (907-960 CE), a time of political fragmentation in China.
  • 直以宋接唐统之正 - Means 'to directly connect the Song dynasty to the legitimate line of the Tang dynasty.'
  • 受周禅与否 - Refers to whether the Song dynasty received its mandate from the Zhou dynasty through abdication.
  • 中遭阳九之阨 - A reference to the 'Nine Yang' (阳九) calamity, a period of great misfortune or disaster.
  • 天犹不泯其社稷 - Means 'heaven still does not abandon the state,' suggesting divine protection.
  • 瓞之系 - A metaphorical phrase meaning 'the continuation of the lineage,' often used to describe the unbroken line of a dynasty.
  • 江之南 - Refers to the southern part of the Yangtze River, often associated with the Song dynasty's southern capital.
  • 子孙享国又凡百五十有五年 - Means 'the descendants enjoyed the state for another 155 years,' emphasizing the longevity of the Song dynasty.
  • 金泰和之议 - Refers to the 'Jin Taihe' (金泰和) period, a time in the Jin dynasty (1115-1234 CE) when discussions were held about the legitimacy of the Song dynasty.
  • 靖康为游魂余魄 - A metaphorical phrase meaning 'the Jingkang period as a ghostly remnant,' suggesting the Song dynasty's weakened state.
  • 昭烈在蜀 - Refers to Liu Bei (刘备), known as 'Emperor Xian of Shu' (昭烈帝), who established the Shu Han state in Sichuan during the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 好党君子遂斥绍兴为伪宋 - Means 'the good party of gentlemen subsequently dismissed the Shaoxing period as a false Song dynasty,' indicating a political dispute over the legitimacy of the Song dynasty.
  • 张昌迎康邸之书 - Refers to Zhang Jiechang's (张昌) letter welcoming the Kangdi (康邸), likely referring to a historical event involving the Song dynasty.
  • 由康邸之旧藩嗣宋朝之大统 - Means 'to succeed the great lineage of the Song dynasty through the old fief of Kangdi,' emphasizing the legitimacy of the Song dynasty's rule.
  • 汉家之戹十世而光武中兴 - Refers to the Han dynasty's revival under Emperor Guangwu (光武帝) after ten generations of decline, a historical analogy.
  • 献公之子九人而重耳尚在 - Refers to the story of Duke Xian of Jin (献公) and his son Chong'er (重耳), who later became Duke Wen of Jin (晋文公), emphasizing the theme of survival and legitimacy.
  • 兹惟天意夫岂人谋 - Means 'this is the will of heaven, not the result of human planning,' attributing the events to divine will.
  • 昌肯以靖康 - Refers to Zhang Jiechang's willingness to acknowledge the Jingkang period.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 生圣人以主生灵自是天人交感而宋祖生矣 - This sentence is elliptical and can be read as 'To give birth to a sage to rule the people, this is the interaction between heaven and humanity, and thus Emperor Taizu of Song was born.'
  • 天厌祸乱之极使之君主中国非欺孤弱寡之所致也 - This clause is complex and can be read as 'Heaven, having grown weary of chaos and disorder, made him ruler of all China, not due to the weakness or isolation of the ruler.'
  • 朱氏纲目于五代之年皆细注于岁之下其余意固有待于宋矣 - This sentence is elliptical and can be read as 'In the 'Zhu Shi Gang Mu,' the Five Dynasties period is detailed in the annual entries, and the rest of the meaning is clearly waiting for the Song dynasty.'
  • 有待于宋则直以宋接唐统之正矣而又何计其受周禅与否乎 - This clause is complex and can be read as 'If it is waiting for the Song dynasty, then it is directly connecting the Song dynasty to the legitimate line of the Tang dynasty, so why should we consider whether it received its mandate from the Zhou dynasty through abdication?'
  • 中遭阳九之阨而天犹不泯其社稷瓞之系在江之南子孙享国又凡百五十有五年 - This sentence is elliptical and can be read as 'In the midst of the Nine Yang calamity, heaven still does not abandon the state, the continuation of the lineage is in the southern part of the Yangtze River, and the descendants enjoyed the state for another 155 years.'
  • 金泰和之议以靖康为游魂余魄比之昭烈在蜀则泰和之议固知宋有遗统在江之左矣而金欲承其絶为得统可乎 - This clause is complex and can be read as 'In the 'Jin Taihe' discussions, the Jingkang period is seen as a ghostly remnant, like Liu Bei in Sichuan, so the 'Jin Taihe' discussions clearly know that the Song dynasty has a remaining lineage in the south, but can the Jin dynasty claim its mandate by succeeding to the Song dynasty's end?'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 其国可灭其史不可灭也 - A rhetorical statement emphasizing that while a nation can be conquered, its history should not be erased.
  • 编年之统在宋矣 - Indicates that the chronological system (or historical continuity) lies with the Song Dynasty.
  • 中华之统正而大者不在辽金而在于天付生灵之主也 - A complex statement asserting that the true and grand lineage of China does not lie with the Liao and Jin Dynasties but with the ruler divinely appointed by heaven.
  • 昭昭矣 - A phrase indicating that something is clearly evident.
  • 接辽以为统 - Means to connect the Liao Dynasty as part of the same lineage.
  • 咈天数之符悖世祖君臣之喻逆万世是非之公论而不恤也 - Refers to defying the signs or indications of heaven's will, going against the teachings or advice of the founding emperor and his ministers, and ignoring the public judgment of right and wrong throughout history.
  • 吁不以天数之正华统之大属之我元承乎有宋如宋之承唐唐之承隋承晋承汉也 - A complex statement arguing that the Yuan Dynasty should be seen as continuing the line of the Song Dynasty, just as the Song continued the Tang, the Tang the Sui, and so on.
  • 妄分闰代之承欲以荒夷非统之统属之我元 - Suggests an attempt to attribute an illegitimate or barbaric lineage to the Yuan Dynasty.
  • 吾又不知今之君子待今日为何时待今圣人为何君也哉 - A rhetorical question expressing disbelief at the current scholars' understanding of the present era and their ruler.

  • 必至武王十有三年代商有天下 - it was only until King Wu's thirteenth year that he replaced Shang and gained the world.
  • 盖革命之事间不容髪 - the matter of revolution leaves no room for hair; metaphorical expression meaning the process of revolution is very tight and precise.
  • 一日之命未絶则一日之统未集 - as long as the mandate of one day is not cut off, the unification of that day is not achieved; emphasizes the immediacy and continuity required in the process of unification.
  • 成周不急文王五十年 - the establishment of the Zhou dynasty did not rush to unify within fifty years of King Wen.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 革命之事间不容髪 - Refers to the concept of revolution in Chinese history, emphasizing its critical nature.
  • 成周不急文王五十年 - Refers to the Zhou dynasty, which did not rush to unify the country after King Wen's death.
  • 道统者治统之所在也 - Refers to the idea that the succession of moral principles (道统) is the foundation of political power (治统).
  • 中立杨氏而吾道南矣 - Refers to the spread of Confucianism to the south during the Song dynasty.
  • 宋亦南渡 - Refers to the Southern Song dynasty, which was established after the fall of the Northern Song.

  • 唐儒萧茂挺能议之 - Means 'the Tang scholar Xiao Maoting was able to discuss this.'

  • 至元十三年丙子春正月十八日 - A classical date format indicating the 13th year of the Zhiyuan era (1276) during the Yuan dynasty, specifically the 18th day of the first month in the spring of the year Bǐzǐ (丙子).
  • 统兵入杭 - Means 'led troops into Hangzhou,' indicating a military action.

  • 曾记得春风雨露玉楼金阙名播兰簪妃后里晕潮莲脸君王侧 - A poetic description of a past life of luxury and favor, using metaphors like 'spring rain and dew,' 'jade towers and golden palaces,' 'lotus faces,' and 'emperor's side.'
  • 忽一朝鼙鼓揭天来緐华歇龙虎散风云灭千古恨慿谁説 - A dramatic shift indicating sudden destruction and loss, with metaphors like 'drums shaking the heavens,' 'luxury ending,' 'dragons and tigers dispersing,' and 'clouds and winds vanishing.'
  • 对山河百二泪霑襟血驿馆夜惊尘土梦宫车晓碾关山月愿嫦娥相顾肯从容随圆缺 - A poignant expression of sorrow and longing, using imagery like 'tears on the chest,' 'nightly dust,' 'palace carriages,' and 'Chang'e's compassion.'
  • 后为女道士 - Means 'later became a female Taoist priestess.'
  • 五月二日抵上都朝见世皇 - Indicates the date and action of arriving at Shangdu to meet the emperor.

  • 乡之人私表曰贞妇著作李五峰先生孝光为记 - Means 'the people of the village privately honored him as a chaste wife and the author, Mr. Li Wufeng, wrote the inscription.'

  • 饮痛甘忘父母讐 - Suggests the emperor's indifference to the suffering of the people and the humiliation of the dynasty.
  • 信使北和怜屈膝 - Refers to the Song dynasty's policy of sending envoys to the Jin dynasty to seek peace, seen as a humiliating act of submission.
  • 策文南驻忍含羞 - Refers to the emperor's policy of remaining in the south while enduring the shame of the dynasty's decline.
  • 两宫五国瞻征帜 - Refers to the two emperors and the five northern states watching the Song dynasty's military banners.
  • 丹诏班师下节楼 - Refers to the emperor's order to withdraw troops from the northern campaign, possibly in response to the Jin dynasty's demands.
  • 万里长城真自壊 - Metaphorical expression suggesting that the Song dynasty's defenses were self-destructed due to internal corruption and complacency.
  • 中兴武绩遂云休 - Refers to the failure of the Song dynasty's efforts to revive its military strength and the cessation of such efforts.
  • 乌乎竟死奸邪手 - Lamentation that the dynasty fell into the hands of treacherous officials, likely referring to Qin Hui and his associates.
  • 颠沛谁为社稷忧 - Rhetorical question asking who would care for the nation in times of turmoil.
  • 黯黯寃魂游狴犴 - Refers to the dark and sorrowful spirits of the fallen dynasty wandering in prison.
  • 纷纷雨泪洒貅 - Refers to the soldiers shedding tears in sorrow.
  • 唯余满地苌血 - Refers to the only remaining traces of the fallen dynasty being the blood of its people.
  • 不见中流祖逖舟 - Refers to the absence of a hero like Zu Ti, who vowed to restore the lost territories of the Jin dynasty.
  • 氛已尘金匼匝 - Refers to the fading glory of the Song dynasty, now reduced to dust.
  • 冕旒终换铁兠鍪 - Refers to the emperor's crown being replaced by the helmet of a common soldier, symbolizing the fall of the dynasty.
  • 姓名竹帛书千载 - Refers to the names of the fallen dynasty's heroes being recorded in history.
  • 父子英雄土一邱 - Refers to the heroes of the dynasty being buried in the same grave, regardless of their familial ties.
  • 老树尚知朝禹穴 - Refers to an old tree still knowing the way to the sacred site of Mount Yuhuang, symbolizing the enduring legacy of the dynasty.
  • 遗总解说王猷 - Refers to the remnants of the dynasty still explaining the deeds of its heroes.
  • 复田起废怜僧寺 - Refers to the revival of the dynasty's lands and the restoration of its temples, symbolizing its rebirth.
  • 移檄褒嘉赖省侯 - Refers to the issuance of edicts to praise the heroes of the dynasty, likely referring to posthumous honors.
  • 圣世即今崇祀典 - Refers to the current era honoring the dynasty's heroes through rituals.
  • 伫看宠渥到松楸 - Refers to the expectation that the honors will reach the graves of the dynasty's heroes.
  • 精忠宋所赐庙额 - Refers to the temple title bestowed upon the heroes of the Song dynasty in recognition of their loyalty.
  • 此诗在未曾加封前作 - Refers to the poem being written before the heroes were officially honored.
  • 时至正己丑也 - Refers to the time period when the poem was written, likely the year 1359 during the Yuan dynasty.
  • 木乃伊 - Refers to a mummy, specifically a preserved body of a 78-year-old man from the Yuan dynasty who chose to be preserved in honey for the benefit of others.
  • 回回田地有年七十八岁老人 - Refers to a 78-year-old man from the Yuan dynasty who chose to be preserved in honey.
  • 絶不饮食惟澡身啖蜜 - Refers to the man's decision to stop eating and drinking, instead bathing and consuming honey.
  • 经月便溺皆蜜 - Refers to the man's bodily functions becoming honey after a month.
  • 既死国人礆以石棺仍满用蜜浸 - Refers to the man's body being placed in a stone coffin and filled with honey after his death.
  • 镌志岁月于棺盖瘗之 - Refers to the man's age and the date of his death being inscribed on the coffin lid and buried.
  • 俟百年后啓封则蜜剂也 - Refers to the man's body being opened after a century, revealing that it had become a honey-based medicine.
  • 凡人损折肢体食少许立愈 - Refers to the belief that consuming a small amount of the man's preserved body could instantly heal injuries.
  • 虽彼中亦不多得 - Refers to the rarity of such preserved bodies in that region.
  • 俗曰蜜人畨言木乃伊 - Refers to the local term for such preserved bodies, which is also known as 'mummy' in foreign languages.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 中一少年曰 - 'Among them was a young man who said...' This is a narrative framing device.
  • 發丘中郎將 - A title, likely referring to a military or official title from the Han dynasty. '發丘' may be a reference to a specific office or rank, possibly related to grave robbing or treasure hunting.
  • 耽耽餓虎 - A metaphor for a ravenous or dangerous tiger, used to describe someone or something that is threatening or insatiable.
  • 事露奈何 - 'What will we do if the matter is exposed?' A rhetorical question expressing concern about exposure.
  • 余固籌矣 - 'I have already planned for this,' a statement of preparedness.
  • 四郊多暴骨 - 'The outskirts are full of exposed bones,' describing widespread death or desecration.
  • 取竄以易 - 'Take them and substitute them,' possibly meaning to replace the remains with others to avoid detection.
  • 誰復知之 - 'Who would know?' A rhetorical question implying the plan will remain secret.
  • 斵文木為匮 - 'Carve a carved wooden chest,' referring to the construction of a container.
  • 復黃絹為囊 - 'Make yellow silk into a bag,' describing the materials used for the containers.
  • 各署其表曰某陵某陵 - 'Each one marked on its label as 'from such-and-such a tomb',' showing a method of disguising the origins.
  • 分委而散遣之 - 'Distribute them and send them away separately,' referring to the dispersal of the containers.
  • 蕝地以藏 - 'Bury them in a secluded place,' indicating a hidden burial site.
  • 為文而告 - 'Write a notice or announcement,' likely a written record of the action.
  • 诘旦事讫来集 - 'At dawn, the matter was completed and they gathered,' marking the conclusion of the action.
  • 出白金羡余 - 'Give out the surplus silver,' referring to distributing the leftover funds.
  • 酬戒勿泄 - 'Reward and warn them not to reveal it,' a way to ensure secrecy.
  • 越七日 - 'After seven days,' marking a time period.
  • 總浮屠下令裒陵骨 - 'The monk (or Buddhist leader) issued an order to collect the bones from the tombs,' indicating a religious or authoritative figure taking action.
  • 雜置牛馬枯骼中 - 'Mix them among the bones of dead cattle and horses,' describing the burial of the remains in an unmarked location.
  • 筑一塔壓之 - 'Build a tower to suppress them,' possibly a symbolic or literal act to bury the remains.
  • 名曰鎮南杭 - 'Named 'Zhen Nanhang',' the name of the tower or site.
  • 民悲戚不忍仰視 - 'The people grieved and could not bear to look up,' describing the emotional impact.
  • 了不知陵骨之猶存也 - 'They did not know that the tomb bones still remained,' indicating the success of the deception.
  • 禍淫不爽 - 'Misfortune does not miss the wicked,' a classical saying from the *Book of Documents* (Shujing), emphasizing divine justice.
  • 天怒赫赫 - 'Heaven’s anger was mighty,' emphasizing divine retribution.
  • 飛風雷號令捽首禍者北焉 - 'The wind and thunder issued commands, and the head of the one who brought calamity was seized and taken north,' describing divine punishment.
  • 由是唐之義風震动吴越 - 'Thus, the righteousness of the Tang family shook Wu and Yue,' referring to the influence of the Tang family’s moral actions.
  • 若胥江掀八月之涛 - 'Like the Xushu River rising in the August tide,' a metaphor for power and momentum.
  • 明年己卯后上元兩日 - 'In the following year, on the two days of the Lantern Festival after the Jǐmǎo year,' referring to a specific time.
  • 良久始蘇曰吾見黃衣吏持文書來告曰王召 - 'After a long time, he awoke and said, 'I saw a yellow-robed official holding a document and saying, 'The King has summoned me.''' This is a vision or dream, possibly of divine judgment or summons.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 發丘中郎將 - A title possibly from the Han dynasty, though not a standard title. It may be a fictional or archaic title used to denote someone in charge of grave robbing or treasure hunting. The term '發丘' may relate to the act of opening graves.

  • 胥江 - A river near Suzhou, known for its strong currents, especially in August. Used metaphorically to describe power and momentum.
  • 上元 - The Lantern Festival, the 15th day of the first lunar month, a major traditional festival.
  • 己卯 - A stem-branch year in the traditional Chinese calendar.
  • 黃衣吏 - A yellow-robed official, possibly a bureaucratic or supernatural figure, often associated with divine or imperial authority in classical Chinese literature.

  • 乃知元命自作多福自求枢机由人虽天有所不能制 - Thus we know that original fate is created by one's own actions, and the pivot and mechanism are controlled by humans, even heaven cannot control it.
  • 圣言岂欺我哉 - The sage's words do not deceive me, emphasizing the trustworthiness of wise sayings.
  • 一分行通神明捷于影响 - Even a slight action can communicate with the divine, swift as shadows and echoes.
  • 况力又有大者其积弥厚其泽当弥长 - Moreover, if the power is greater, its accumulation becomes deeper, and its influence will last longer.
  • 又可以槩量乎哉 - Can it be measured? Expressing the idea that such things are beyond measurement.
  • 吾谓赵氏昔者家已破程婴公孙杵臼强有其真孤 - I say that the Zhao family, once broken, was protected by Cheng Ying and Gong Sun Chujiu, who bravely saved the true heir.
  • 今者国已亡唐君玉潜匮藏其真骨 - Now, the state has fallen, and Tang Junyu secretly stored the true bones.
  • 两雄力当无能优劣以其系人伦关世教有足多尚援笔以纪待编野史者采焉 - The two heroes were equally strong, but their actions were significant because they related to human relationships and world teachings, worthy of recording for future historians to compile.
  • 此云溪罗先生有开所撰也 - This was written by Luo Xian, known as Yunki Youkai.

  • 在下风精诚动天奇节震世锡佳丽偶送麒麟儿阳施隂报捷若影响善者劝矣 - Elliptical phrase combining several concepts: sincerity moving heaven, extraordinary virtue shaking the world, receiving a beautiful child, sending a qilin (a mythical creature symbolizing good fortune), and positive actions leading to swift rewards, which encourages virtue.
  • 詹厚斋载道复题曰 - Means 'Zhan Houzhi, carrying the Dao, replied and inscribed.'
  • 尝疑武王伐商劒钺斩击事 - Means 'I once doubted the matter of King Wu of Zhou's attack on the Shang with swords and axes.'
  • 窃意王者之师未必尔也 - Means 'I secretly thought that the army of a king might not be like that.'
  • 纣死矣既击之又断其首注太白不已甚乎 - Means 'After King Zhou died, they struck his head off and then struck it again, and this was too excessive.'
  • 当时举天下无非之者而西山饿夫独非之 - Means 'At that time, the whole world criticized it, but the starving man from the West Mountain alone criticized it.'
  • 昌颂之曰若伯夷者特立独行穷天地亘万古而不顾者也 - Means 'Chang Si praised him, saying that someone like Bo Yi stands alone and acts independently, transcending the heavens and earth and enduring through all ages without regard.'
  • 会稽诸陵非有商辛之虐不幸而遭樊崇当时曾无一人动孟阳之哀者呜呼痛哉 - Means 'The tombs at Kuaiji did not have the cruelty of Shang Xin, but unfortunately, they were destroyed by Fan Chong, and at that time, there was not a single person who moved by the sorrow of Mengyang; alas, how tragic!'
  • 唐生一寒士耳其埶位非如孤竹君之子徒以故国遗不忍视其上之人之祸之惨愤激于中毁家取义为人所不敢为于不可为之时深谋秘计全而归之智名勇功足以惊世绝俗视伯夷固未易同日语而一念之烈行之而不顾岂非韩子所谓千百年乃一人 - Means 'Tang was just a poor scholar; his status was not like that of the son of the Duke of Guzhu. He only acted out of love for his old country, unable to bear to see the suffering of those above, and was filled with anger. He sacrificed his family for righteousness, doing what others dared not do, at a time when it was impossible to do so. He devised deep and secret plans and returned safely, achieving wisdom, courage, and deeds that could shock the world and surpass the common. Compared to Bo Yi, he is not easily mentioned in the same breath, but his determination and actions are such that they are indeed the kind of thing that happens once in a thousand years, as Hanzi said.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '在下风精诚动天奇节震世锡佳丽偶送麒麟儿阳施隂报捷若影响善者劝矣' is highly elliptical and combines several concepts, making it challenging to translate directly. It suggests a series of events and their moral implications.
  • The phrase '载道' (carrying the Dao) is a metaphor for carrying a moral or philosophical message.
  • The phrase '窃意' (secretly thought) introduces a personal reflection on the historical event.
  • The phrase '注太白不已甚乎' (struck it again, and this was too excessive) emphasizes the excessive nature of the action.
  • The phrase '独非之' (alone criticized it) highlights the uniqueness of Bo Yi's criticism.
  • The phrase '特立独行' (stands alone and acts independently) emphasizes Bo Yi's moral integrity.
  • The phrase '呜呼痛哉' (alas, how tragic) expresses deep sorrow.
  • The long sentence describing Tang's actions and their moral significance, emphasizing his determination and the rarity of such actions.

  • 公曰美则美矣若改山为天野为星则尤美 - Conditional statement suggesting that if certain changes are made (changing 'mountain' to 'heaven' and 'wilderness' to 'stars'), the text would be even more beautiful.
  • 虞先生深服之 - Mr. Yu deeply admired or agreed with the suggestion.

  • 既婚之三日即窃谓其夫曰观君之才貌非久在人后者何不为去计而甘心于此乎 - After three days of marriage, the woman secretly told her husband that given his talent and appearance, he should not remain a slave and should consider escaping.
  • 夫疑其试已也诉于张张命棰之 - The husband doubted her sincerity and reported her to Zhang, who ordered her to be beaten.
  • 越三日复告曰君若去必可成大器否则终为人奴耳 - After three more days, she told him again that if he left, he could achieve great things, otherwise he would remain a slave.
  • 夫愈疑之又诉于张张命出之遂粥于市人家妻临行以所穿绣鞵一易程一履泣而曰期执此相见矣 - The husband doubted her even more and reported her again, leading Zhang to expel her. She was then sold to a common family. As she left, she gave him one of her embroidered shoes in exchange for one of his, crying that they would meet again with these shoes.
  • 程感悟奔归宋时年十七八以防补入官迨国朝统一海宇程为陜西行省参知政事自与妻别已三十余年义其为人未尝再娶至是遣人擕向之鞵履往 - Gong was moved by her words and fled back to Song when he was in his late teens. He later entered the government and became a vice-provincial governor in Shaanxi. After more than thirty years, he sent someone to bring back the shoes and sandals they had exchanged.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 无恙 - Classical expression meaning 'safe and sound' or 'without illness'; used both literally and figuratively.
  • 岁无恙耶 - Is the year safe and sound? A way of asking about the general condition of the year, often implying the harvest or state of the country.
  • 王亦无恙耶 - Is the king also safe and sound? A polite inquiry about the health or safety of the ruler.
  • 击无恙乎 - Are you unharmed? A direct question to someone, likely an animal or person, inquiring about their well-being.
  • 子之君无恙乎 - Is your lord safe and sound? A polite way of inquiring about the well-being of someone else's ruler or superior.
  • 乞骸骨 - A euphemism for requesting to retire or resign, literally meaning 'to ask for one's bones to be returned to the earth' (i.e., to die or retire).
  • 公问君賔对公再拜 - A formal ritual exchange in which the host (公) asks the guest (君賔) about their well-being, and the guest responds with a bow (再拜).
  • 布帆无恙 - The sail is unharmed, a metaphor for a journey or travel proceeding smoothly.
  • 致书 - To send a letter or message, often used in diplomatic or official contexts.
  • 无恙神异经 - A reference to a classical text, *Shen Yi Jing* (The Book of Strange Things), which contains mythical and supernatural accounts.
  • 防防恙也 - A compound term, possibly a name or a description of a mythical beast, with the word **恙** (illness) used in a symbolic or metaphorical way.
  • 无忧疾谓之无恙 - Without worry or illness, it is called *wúyàng*.
  • 恙忧也 - The definition of **恙** as 'worry' or 'distress.'
  • 噬人虫 - A 'bug that bites humans,' often used in classical texts to describe dangerous insects.
  • 善食人心 - Good at eating human hearts, describing a particularly dangerous or malevolent creature.
  • 广干禄书 - A classical text, *Guan Lu Shu* (The Book of Broadening Official Rank), which discusses how to attain official positions and success.
  • 事物纪原 - A classical text, *Shi Wu Ji Yuan* (The Origin of Things), which seeks to trace the origins of various phenomena.
  • 广韵 - *Guang Yun*, a classical Chinese rhyme dictionary, used to determine the phonetic and semantic properties of characters.
  • 防字下云防兽如狮子 - 'Under the character *fang*, it says that the *fang* beast is like a lion,' referring to an entry in *Guang Yun*.
  • 恙字下云忧也病也 - 'Under the character *yang*, it says it means 'worry' or 'illness.'
  • 噬虫善食人心 - The biting insect that eats human hearts, a description of a dangerous creature.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 即月大也】月小分明只廿九【如月朔数至次月朔止廿九日即月小也】 - Explanation of the lunar calendar: 'this month is a full month,' 'a small month is clearly only twenty-nine days,' and 'if the number of days from the first day of one month to the first day of the next month is twenty-nine, then it is a small month.'
  • 节气只凭九年厯【假造甲午年厯则看丙戌年节气】 - 'The solar terms depend solely on the nine-year calendar,' and 'if you create a calendar for the year Jia Wu, you should look at the solar terms of the year Bing Xu.'
  • 二十四气真端的【要知今年节气则看前九年中是何节气】 - 'The twenty-four solar terms are truly accurate,' and 'to know the solar terms of this year, look at what they were in the previous nine years.'
  • 天干三数地支七【假如癸亥日癸见乙三数亥见己七数也】 - 'The heavenly stems are three and the earthly branches are seven,' and 'for example, on the day Gui Hai, Gui corresponds to Yi (three) and Hai corresponds to Ji (seven).'
  • 熟记心中须歴歴定时二十四年取逢时遇八君无虑【如逢子时交节气却用未时亦交也中气如之】 - 'Memorize it well in your heart, and the timing will be clear after twenty-four years, meeting the eighth time without worry,' and 'if you encounter the Zi hour for the solar term, use the Wei hour instead, and it will also be correct for the middle term.'
  • 若依此法个中推方省隂阳奥处闰月本来中气无【古云闰月无中气】 - 'If you follow this method, you can understand the profound aspects of yin and yang, and the leap month originally has no middle term,' and 'it is said that a leap month has no middle term.'
  • 何劳物外更他图世人谙得神仙术不是愚氓是丈夫 - 'Why bother with other things outside the world?' and 'those who understand the art of immortality are not fools but men of great virtue.'
  • 又歌曰九年二月半便是正月一【前九年二月十五日辰即今年正月初一日辰 - 'Nine years ago, the middle of February is the first day of January this year,' and 'nine years ago, the fifteenth day of February at the hour of Chen is the first day of January this year at the hour of Chen.'

  • 闰月无中气说与惺惺人 - Refers to the rule that a leap month must contain a 'middle solar term' (zhong qi), and it is directed at the 'enlightened' (xing xing ren), meaning those who understand the system.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 斷十二日足則復起一數焉 - Formulaic expression indicating that after counting twelve days, one begins the count again from the beginning.
  • 推節氣歌括 - Term referring to a mnemonic or poetic formula used to calculate or predict the solar terms (節氣) in the traditional Chinese lunisolar calendar.
  • 云中氣與節氣但有半月隔 - Phrase indicating that the solar term (氣) and the corresponding solar term (節氣) are separated by only half a month (approximately 15 days).
  • 要知仔細兩時零五刻 - Precise time measurement: 'two hours and five *ke*' (a traditional Chinese unit of time).
  • 謂如正月甲子日子時初初刻立春 - Example sentence illustrating the method of calculation, using the date and time of the solar term 'Lichun' (立春) on the first day of the first lunar month (正月) on the day of *Jiazi* (甲子), at *zi* hour and the very first *ke*.
  • 推立春歌括云 - Another mnemonic or formula used to calculate the date and time of *Lichun* (立春), the first solar term of the year.
  • 今歲先知來歲春 - Formulaic expression meaning 'knowing this year’s *Lichun* allows one to predict next year’s *Lichun*.'
  • 但隔五日三时辰 - Indicates that the next year’s *Lichun* occurs five days and three *shi* (hours) later than the previous year’s.
  • 若夫刻數則用前法推之 - Phrase indicating that the calculation of *ke* (minutes) follows the same method used for days and hours.
  • 又求節氣歌曰 - Introduces another mnemonic or poetic formula for calculating other solar terms.
  • 驚蟄五時二刻 - Indicates that the solar term *Jingzhe* (驚蟄) occurs at five hours and two *ke* (minutes) after a certain reference point.
  • 清明十時四刻 - Indicates that the solar term *Qingming* (清明) occurs at ten hours and four *ke* after a reference point.
  • 流立夏一日三時六 - Phrase likely refers to the solar term *Lixia* (立夏) occurring one day, three *shi* (hours), and six *ke* (minutes) after a reference point.
  • 芒種一日九時周 - Indicates that the solar term *Mangzhong* (芒種) occurs one day, nine *shi* (hours), and possibly *zhou* (周), which may be a typo or a term for a unit of time.
  • 小暑二日二時二 - Indicates that the solar term *Xiaoshu* (小暑) occurs two days, two *shi* (hours), and two *ke* (minutes) after a reference point.
  • 立秋二日七時四 - Indicates that the solar term *Liqiu* (立秋) occurs two days, seven *shi* (hours), and four *ke* (minutes) after a reference point.
  • 白露三日零六刻 - Indicates that the solar term *Bailu* (白露) occurs three days and six *ke* (minutes) after a reference point.
  • 寒露三日六時至 - Indicates that the solar term *Hanlu* (寒露) occurs three days, six *shi* (hours), and possibly *zhi* (至), which may be a typo or a term for a unit of time.
  • 立冬三日十一二 - Indicates that the solar term *Lidong* (立冬) occurs three days, eleven or twelve *shi* (hours) after a reference point.
  • 大雪四日四時四 - Indicates that the solar term *Daxue* (大雪) occurs four days, four *shi* (hours), and four *ke* (minutes) after a reference point.
  • 小寒四日九時六 - Indicates that the solar term *Xiaohan* (小寒) occurs four days, nine *shi* (hours), and six *ke* (minutes) after a reference point.
  • 五日三時交新歲節 - Indicates that the solar term marking the beginning of the new year occurs five days and three *shi* (hours) after a reference point.
  • 遇子時加一日 - Indicates that if the calculation falls on the *zi* hour (子時), one additional day should be added.
  • 此為捷法君須記 - Closing phrase meaning 'this is a quick method; you must remember it.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 節氣 - Refers to the 24 solar terms in the traditional Chinese lunisolar calendar, which mark the progression of the seasons and are used for agricultural and ritual purposes.
  • 立春 - The first solar term of the year, marking the beginning of spring.
  • 驚蟄 - The third solar term, indicating the time when insects awaken from hibernation.
  • 清明 - The fifth solar term, associated with the Qingming Festival, a time for ancestor worship.
  • 立夏 - The fourth solar term, marking the beginning of summer.
  • 芒種 - The sixth solar term, associated with the sowing of grain.
  • 小暑 - The tenth solar term, indicating the beginning of the hot summer season.
  • 立秋 - The seventh solar term, marking the beginning of autumn.
  • 白露 - The fifteenth solar term, indicating the time when dew appears on grass.
  • 寒露 - The sixteenth solar term, indicating the time when the dew becomes cold.
  • 立冬 - The nineteenth solar term, marking the beginning of winter.
  • 大雪 - The twentieth solar term, associated with heavy snowfall.
  • 小寒 - The twenty-first solar term, indicating the beginning of the coldest season.
  • 新歲節 - Likely refers to the solar term marking the start of the new year, possibly *Lichun* (立春) or another term depending on the calendar system.
  • 子時 - The first of the 12 two-hour time divisions in the traditional Chinese time system, corresponding to midnight (23:00–1:00).
  • 甲子 - A day in the traditional Chinese sexagenary cycle (60-day cycle), consisting of combinations of the ten heavenly stems and twelve earthly branches.

  • 如过初十于千上下数 - Statement about the method of counting for days beyond the tenth.

  • 则先下一千一百数十一日故也 - Statement about the initial step in the calculation.
  • 再下十二数亥时故也 - Statement about the next step in the calculation.
  • 如子一丑二之 - Statement about the specific hours and their corresponding numbers.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 一年约法 - Refers to the traditional Chinese method of measuring time, which is based on the solar calendar.
  • 二十四气 - Refers to the 24 solar terms in the traditional Chinese calendar, which are used to mark the progression of the seasons.
  • 食神 - Refers to a concept in traditional Chinese astrology, where certain days are considered auspicious or inauspicious based on the elements of the day.
  • 时刻约法歌 - Refers to a traditional Chinese poem or song that describes the method of calculating the moments of the 24 solar terms.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 一年约法云一周年三百六十五日零三时一月节三十日零五时二刻半月一气十五日零二时五刻 - This long sequence must be read as a single statement about the precise lengths of a year, month, and half-month.
  • 食神定法云甲食丙乙食丁丙食戊丁食己戊食庚巳食辛庚食壬辛食癸壬食甲癸食乙 - This long sequence must be read as a single statement about the rule for determining the food star based on the elements of the day.
  • 时刻约法歌云二十四气渐差除循环时刻四同途单逢正四换初一正三依旧复初初 - This long sequence must be read as a single statement about the gradual differences in the 24 solar terms and their cyclical nature.
  • 乘除法推算二十四气时刻云其法不论何岁何月但以日为百数时为十数刻为零数初一至初十于百上下数如过初十于千上下数假如正月十一日亥正一刻立春欲求中气则先下一千一百数十一日故也再下十二数亥时故也如子一丑二之 - This long sequence must be read as a single statement about the method of calculating the moments of the 24 solar terms, using days as the base unit.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 则二十七日寅初二刻雨水也 - The calculation results in the date and time of the Rain Water solar term on the 27th day at the second watch of the hour of Yin.
  • 何以知为初二刻 - How do we know it is the second watch, referring to the method of determining the specific time.

  • 此立成法也 - This is a standard method, indicating that the method is well-established.
  • 今零三数乃是初二刻矣 - Now, the third number is the second watch, indicating the specific time based on the calculation.
  • 欲求二月节则于前数上加一五二五 - To find the second month's solar term, add 1525 to the previous number, indicating a continuation of the calculation.
  • 即前一千五百二十五也 - The number 1525 is the same as previously mentioned.

  • 除去二十九日 - Subtract 29 days, referring to the subtraction of the number of days in a month.
  • 如遇大月除去三十日算中气则不除大小月 - If it is a large month, subtract 30 days; when calculating mid-solar terms, do not subtract for large or small months, indicating the adjustment for different month lengths.
  • 剩下一三五八则十三日辰时正三刻惊蛰也 - The remainder is 1358, which is the 13th day at the third watch of the hour of Chen for the Awakening of Insects solar term, indicating the final result of the calculation.
  • 余仿此 - The rest follow this pattern, indicating that the same method applies to other calculations.
  • 十二时为一日 - 12 hours make up a day, referring to the traditional Chinese time system.

  • 八刻为一时 - 8 watches make up an hour, referring to the division of an hour into 8 watches.
  • 如遇九刻以上则退八刻为一时 - If it is 9 watches or more, subtract 8 watches to get the hour, indicating the adjustment for watches exceeding 8.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 提刑节制水军 - 提刑 refers to a high-ranking official in charge of judicial and military affairs; 节制水军 means to command or regulate the naval forces. Together, this phrase refers to the official’s authority over the navy.
  • 江南既内附 - 内附 means to submit or surrender; this phrase indicates that the southern Jiangnan region had already surrendered to the Mongol forces.
  • 授金符千戸 - 金符 is a golden token or seal used as a symbol of authority; 千戸 is a rank or position in the Mongol military system, roughly equivalent to a thousand-household commander.
  • 方挽漕东南供京师 - 挽漕 refers to the act of hauling or transporting grain via canals; 东南 is the southeast region; 供京师 means to supply the capital city (Dadu, the Yuan capital). This phrase describes the logistical effort of transporting grain from the southeast to the capital.
  • 运河隘浅不容大舟 - 隘浅 means narrow and shallow; 容大舟 means unable to accommodate large ships. This refers to the difficulty of navigating the Grand Canal due to its narrow and shallow conditions.
  • 不能百里五十里輙为堰潴水 - 輙为堰潴水 means that after every 50 or 100 li (miles), they would build dams to hold water. This describes the practice of constructing temporary dams to manage water levels in the canal.
  • 絶江淮遡泗水吕梁彭城 - 絶 means to block or cut off; 遡 means to go upstream; 泗水吕梁彭城 refers to the rivers and locations along the route. This phrase indicates the logistical challenge of transporting goods upstream against the current.
  • 会通河未凿 - 会通河 is a specific canal; 未凿 means not yet excavated. This refers to the fact that the canal had not yet been constructed.
  • 东阿茌平道中车运三百里转输艰而糜费重 - This phrase describes the difficulty of transporting goods over 300 li (miles) by cart along the road between Dong’e and Chizheng, emphasizing the high cost and inefficiency.
  • 建言海漕事试之良便 - 建言 means to propose or recommend; 海漕 is sea transport; 试之良便 means it was tested and found to be convenient. This refers to the proposal to use sea transport instead of the canal.
  • 父子致位宰相 - 致位 means to reach a position; 宰相 is the prime minister or chief minister. This indicates that both father and son attained the position of prime minister.
  • 弟侄甥壻皆大官 - This lists the family members (younger brothers, nephews, sons-in-law) who all held high-ranking positions.
  • 田园宅馆徧天下 - 徧天下 means spread throughout the country. This describes the widespread land and property owned by the family.
  • 库藏仓庾相望 - 库藏 means warehouses; 仓庾 are granaries; 相望 means visible from one another. This emphasizes the abundance of stored goods.
  • 巨艘大舶帆交番夷中 - 巨艘大舶 are large ships; 帆交 means sails overlapping; 番夷 refers to foreign peoples or lands. This describes the fleet of large ships sailing among foreign territories.
  • 舆骑塞隘门巷左右仆从皆佩于莵金符为万户千戸累爵积赀气意自得 - 舆骑塞隘 means carriages and horses filled the narrow streets; 左右仆从 are attendants; 佩于莵金符 refers to wearing golden tokens (the 金符); 为万户千戸 refers to holding ranks of thousand-household commander; 累爵积赀 means accumulating titles and wealth; 气意自得 means feeling self-assured and proud.
  • 既满盈父子同时夷戮殆尽 - 夷戮 means to be executed; 殆尽 means nearly all were eliminated. This describes how the family was overthrown and their members executed.
  • 没赀产县官党与家破禁锢 - 没赀产 means confiscation of property; 县官党与 refers to officials and allies; 家破禁锢 means the family was ruined and its members imprisoned.
  • 大徳六年冬也 - 大德 is the reign name of Emperor Chengzong of Yuan; 六年冬 means the winter of the sixth year. This is a specific historical date.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This sentence ranks calligraphy collections in order of quality, with the *Jue* (阁) collection being the best, followed by the *Jiang* (绛) collection, then *Linjiang* (临江), *Tan* (潭), *Wugang* (武冈), and *Daguan* (大观) being the most exquisite.
  • This sentence compares the quality of the *Wugang* and *Jiang* collections, stating that the best copies of *Wugang* can be mistaken for *Jiang*, and the best copies of *Linjiang* can be mistaken for *Jue*. It also mentions that the *Tan* collection was copied by a monk named Xi Bai and has a style reminiscent of the eastern part of China (*Jiangzuo*).
  • This sentence evaluates Xi Bai's skill in copying calligraphy, noting that he is skilled at copying characters but lacks precision in the meticulous process of copying (*xunxing shumo*), leading to textual errors. Despite his skill, the text is difficult to read.
  • This sentence compares the quality of the *Tan* collection to the *Chunhua* collection, stating that the characters in the *Tan* collection are superior.
  • This sentence mentions that Su Shi (东坡) believed the *Tan* collection was superior to the *Jue* collection.
  • This sentence mentions that Han Tuozhou's collection, the *Qunyu* (群玉) collection, had good calligraphy.
  • This sentence mentions that Xue Shaoping also had a good calligraphy collection associated with a private school or family collection.
  • This sentence describes Liu Yanqing's account of viewing the original stone versions of the *Chunhua* collection with his father-in-law Zhang Junxi in 1309 CE.
  • This sentence mentions that the first volume of the *Chunhua* collection was inscribed by Fan Zhongyan (范仲淹), a famous statesman and scholar, with the date of his viewing.
  • This sentence mentions that the fifth volume of the *Chunhua* collection was inscribed by Su Shi (东坡) and Zhang Wenzhen (张文潜), among others.
  • This sentence mentions that the sixth volume of the *Chunhua* collection was inscribed by an unknown person from Luoyang (洛阳) and Yichuan (伊川).
