  • 偶讀劉防暇日記亦載此事雲馬傳慶説此帖本唐保大年摹上石題雲保大七年倉曹防軍王文炳摹勒校對無差國朝下江南得此石淳化中太宗令將書館所有増作十卷為版本而石本復以火斷缺人家時收得一二卷然閣帖於各卷尾書題雲淳 - This is another long, complex sentence that provides additional historical context and details about the calligraphy collection. It can be broken down as follows: '偶讀劉防暇日記亦載此事' means 'by chance, I read Liu Fang's diary of leisure and it also records this matter'; '雲馬傳慶説' means 'it says that Ma Chuanqing said'; '此帖本唐保大年摹上石' means 'this calligraphy collection originally from the Baoying year of the Tang dynasty was copied and inscribed onto stone'; '題雲保大七年倉曹防軍王文炳摹勒校對無差' means 'the inscription reads 'the seventh year of the Baoying era, the military officer Wang Wenbing, the copying, engraving, and proofreading were all correct'; '國朝下江南得此石' means 'our dynasty conquered the south and obtained this stone'; '淳化中太宗令將書館所有増作十卷為版本而石本復以火斷缺' means 'in the Chunhua period, Emperor Taizong ordered the library to add ten volumes to create a model version, but the stone version was damaged by fire'; '人家時收得一二卷' means 'people at the time collected one or two volumes'; '然閣帖於各卷尾書題雲淳' means 'however, the library's version has a note at the end of each volume saying 'Chun.''

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 令焉 - Formal introductory particle, similar to 'indeed' or 'therefore,' used to emphasize the following statement.
  • 降俗薄悖逆其父母者 - Compound phrase describing people who have abandoned filial piety and rebelled against their parents. '降俗' means 'to lower one's morals,' '薄' means 'to be lacking,' and '悖逆' means 'to rebel against.'
  • 视良吉何如哉 - Rhetorical question asking how someone like Liang Ji compares to others. '视' means 'to see' or 'to compare,' '何如' means 'how is it,' and '哉' is a particle used to emphasize the question.
  • 自当旌异为世教劝 - Phrase meaning 'should naturally be honored and praised as a model for the world to learn from.' '自当' means 'should naturally,' '旌异' means 'to honor and praise,' and '为世教劝' means 'as a model for the world to learn from.'
  • 有司曾莫能省原 - Phrase meaning 'the officials have never been able to understand or appreciate.' '有司' refers to government officials, '曾' means 'never,' '莫能' means 'unable to,' and '省原' means 'understand or appreciate.'
  • 一念之纯 - Phrase meaning 'the purity of a single thought' or 'a pure intention.' '一念' means 'a single thought,' and '纯' means 'pure.'
  • 剖心之际 - Phrase meaning 'at the moment of sincerity' or 'when one's heart is truly revealed.' '剖心' means 'to reveal one's heart,' and '之际' means 'at the moment of.'
  • 不待赏之于有司 - Phrase meaning 'without waiting for recognition from the officials.' '不待' means 'without waiting,' '赏' means 'to reward,' and '于有司' means 'from the officials.'
  • 太上感应篇所谓 - Phrase meaning 'as stated in the *Tai Shang Yin Ying Pian* (a Daoist text).' '太上' means 'the Supreme One,' '感应' means 'cause and effect,' and '篇' means 'chapter' or 'section.'
  • 今日谢世明日为地下主进补仙阶 - Phrase meaning 'today he leaves the world, and tomorrow he becomes the master of the underworld and advances to a higher rank among the immortals.' '谢世' means 'to leave the world,' '地下主' means 'master of the underworld,' '进补' means 'to advance,' and '仙阶' means 'rank among the immortals.'
  • 故为显白其孝以为人子之劝省也 - Phrase meaning 'therefore, to make his filial piety clear and serve as an example for others to learn from.' '显白' means 'to make clear,' '孝' means 'filial piety,' '为人子之劝省' means 'to serve as an example for others to learn from.'
  • 人多传道 - Phrase meaning 'many people have passed it down.' '人多' means 'many people,' '传道' means 'to pass down' or 'to spread.'
  • 元故白云漫士陶明元氏讳煜 - Phrase meaning 'Mr. Tao Mingyuan, a former White Cloud Scholar, whose courtesy name was Yu.' '元故' means 'former,' '白云漫士' is the title 'White Cloud Scholar,' '陶明元氏' is the surname 'Tao' and the name 'Mingyuan,' and '讳煜' means 'whose courtesy name was Yu.'
  • 弱冠时 - Phrase meaning 'when he was young, around the age of 20.' '弱冠' means 'the age of 20.'
  • 明元母病心痛 - Phrase meaning 'Mingyuan's mother suffered from heart pain.' '明元母' means 'Mingyuan's mother,' '病' means 'suffered from,' and '心痛' means 'heart pain.'
  • 痛则拍张 - Phrase meaning 'she would clap her hands.' '痛则' means 'when she was in pain,' and '拍张' means 'to clap her hands.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 令焉 - A formal particle used to introduce a statement, often found in classical texts.
  • 降俗薄悖逆其父母者 - Refers to people who have abandoned filial piety, a core value in Confucian ethics.
  • 视良吉何如哉 - A rhetorical question emphasizing the exceptional nature of Liang Ji's filial piety.
  • 自当旌异为世教劝 - Reflects the Confucian ideal of using exemplary behavior to teach and inspire others.
  • 有司曾莫能省原 - Criticizes the failure of officials to recognize and appreciate virtuous behavior.
  • 一念之纯 - Highlights the importance of pure intentions in Confucian thought.
  • 剖心之际 - A metaphor for revealing one's true self, often used in Daoist and Confucian contexts.
  • 不待赏之于有司 - Emphasizes that true virtue is recognized by heaven and the spirits, not just by human officials.
  • 太上感应篇所谓 - Refers to the *Tai Shang Yin Ying Pian*, a Daoist text that emphasizes the principle of cause and effect.
  • 今日谢世明日为地下主进补仙阶 - Suggests that Liang Ji's filial piety will lead to a higher status in the afterlife, a common belief in both Daoist and Confucian traditions.
  • 故为显白其孝以为人子之劝省也 - Emphasizes the importance of setting an example for others to follow, a key Confucian principle.
  • 人多传道 - Indicates that the story has been widely passed down, suggesting its significance.

  • 明元之事遂昌郑元祐状行防稽先生杨维桢志墓皆不书 - The deeds of Ming Yuan were recorded by Zheng Yuanyou in his memorial for the tomb of Yang Weizhen, a scholar from Shaoxing, but they were not written down.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This sentence is complex and requires careful parsing. It begins with a general statement about the *Zuo Zhuan* and then shifts to specific details about Ming Yuan's deeds being omitted in a memorial.
  • This sentence is a long, complex statement that criticizes the God of the North's abilities, comparing them unfavorably to those of legendary physicians.
  • This sentence is a rhetorical question that questions the existence of a 'heavenly physician' and suggests a supernatural explanation for filial piety.
  • This sentence is a conclusion that provides a moral reflection and the author's name. It requires careful parsing to understand the relationships between the different clauses.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 者悬腰间不置也遂用伪献留不还 can be interpreted as 'he hung it around his waist and did not put it down, so he used it as a false offering to stay and not return.'
  • 谓曰可呼汝女行酒以疾辞大王怒便欲为奸意 can be broken down into: 'He said to call your daughter to serve wine, but she excused herself due to illness, which angered the king and led him to have malicious intentions.'
  • 妪窃思道流之说计嵗数又合乃解所佩铁简投酒灶火内既而电掣雷轰烟火满室须臾平息击死猕猴数十其一最钜疑即向之逃者所赍随行器用悉系金银宝玉赴告有司籍入官库 can be broken down into: 'The old woman secretly thought about the doctrine, counted the years, and then threw her iron tablet into the fire in the wine stove. Lightning and thunder followed, filling the room with smoke and fire. After a short while, the fire subsided, killing dozens of monkeys, one of which was particularly large, suspected to be the one that had escaped. The old woman reported this to the authorities, and all the valuables (gold and silver) were recorded and stored in the official treasury.'
  • 台哈布哈元师为西台御史日阅其案朱语曰鬼魅云余亲闻台公说甚详且有钞具案文惜不随即记录今则忘邑里姓名岁月矣 can be broken down into: 'Tai Ha Bu Ha Yuan, a Western Platform Censor, read the case and made a red annotation saying it is a ghost story. I personally heard this from Tai Ha Bu Ha Yuan in detail, and there is a written record of the case, but it was not recorded at the time, and now the details are forgotten.'
  • 今人以居士自号者甚多考之六经中惟礼记玉藻有曰居士锦带注谓道艺处士也吴曾能改斋漫录云居士之号起于商周之时按韩非子书曰太公封于齐东海上有居士任矞华仕昆弟二人立议曰吾不臣天子不友诸侯耕而食之掘而饮之吾无求于人无上之名无君之禄不仕而事力云然则居士云者处士之类是已 can be broken down into: 'Many people today call themselves '居士.' Examining the Six Classics, only the *Book of Rites* in the *Yuzao* chapter mentions '居士' as someone who wears a brocade belt. Wu Zeng's *Gai Zhai Man Lu* states that the term '居士' originated during the Shang and Zhou dynasties. According to the *Han Feizi*, when Tai Gong was enfeoffed in Qi, there were two brothers named Ren Yu Hua who declared that they would not serve the emperor, not befriend the feudal lords, but instead farm for food and dig for water. They had no desires for others, no titles, no salaries, and did not seek official positions. Thus, '居士' refers to a type of recluse.'

Proper Nouns

  • 台哈布哈元师 - A historical figure, possibly a censor or official.
  • 西台御史 - A high-ranking official in the imperial court.
  • 礼记玉藻 - A specific chapter in the *Book of Rites*.
  • 吴曾能改斋漫录 - A historical text by Wu Zeng, a scholar from the Song dynasty.
  • 韩非子书 - The *Han Feizi*, a philosophical text by Han Fei.
  • 太公 - Refers to Tai Gong, a historical figure often associated with the founding of the State of Qi.
  • 任矞华 - One of the brothers mentioned in the *Han Feizi*.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 官奴 - A term for a male servant in official service, literally 'official slave.' In Classical Chinese, it refers to state-owned servants, often of lower status.
  • 妓為官奴即官婢也 - This phrase equates 'female entertainers' (妓) to 'official slaves' (官婢), indicating that in the context of state control, female entertainers are treated as state servants or property.
  • 周禮天官酒人奚三百人 - A reference to the *Rites of Zhou* (*Zhouli*), where '天官' refers to the 'Heavenly Officials' or the central administrative system. '酒人' is the official in charge of wine, and '奚' refers to laborers or servants. The phrase '奚三百人' is a classical enumeration of 300 laborers.
  • 注今之待史官婢 - A note or commentary stating that the '奚' in the *Rites of Zhou* correspond to '待史官婢' (female attendants or servants in the office of the historian).
  • 右此石是南唐寳石 - '右' is a classical preface marker meaning 'the following' or 'the above.' '此石' refers to 'this stone,' and '南唐寳石' is 'a treasure stone from the Southern Tang dynasty.'
  • 研山不易見 - A poetic expression meaning 'the Yanshan is not easily seen' or 'the Yanshan is rare to behold.'
  • 移得小翠峯 - 'To move a small emerald peak' – a metaphor for acquiring a rare and beautiful stone.
  • 潤色裛書幾 - 'To moisten and polish the writing desk' – a poetic way of saying the stone enhances the beauty of writing or calligraphy.
  • 觸目萬慮空 - 'Touching the eyes, all thoughts vanish' – a poetic expression of mental clarity or transcendence.
  • 公家富竒石 - 'The public house is rich in strange stones' – a metaphor for the state or officialdom possessing rare and valuable stones.
  • 不許常人同 - 'Not allowed to be shared with common people' – indicating exclusivity or restriction of access.
  • 立壁照春野 - 'Standing cliff reflecting the spring fields' – a poetic description of the stone's form and its interaction with nature.
  • 當有千丈松 - 'There should be a thousand-foot pine' – a metaphor for grandeur and strength.
  • 偃仰蟠蛟龍 - 'Bowing and rising, coiled dragons' – a poetic image of the stone's shape resembling mythical creatures.
  • 塵夢忽不到 - 'Dust dreams suddenly do not come' – a metaphor for the stone's ability to elevate the mind above mundane concerns.
  • 美与众物肩 - 'Beautiful and equal to all things' – a poetic way of saying the stone is as beautiful as any natural object.
  • 哦詩徒叹息 - 'Reciting poetry, only sighs' – an expression of melancholy or longing.
  • 唯有玉蟾蜍向余頻淚滴 - 'Only the jade toad frequently weeps toward me' – a poetic metaphor for the emotional connection between the writer and the stone.
  • 此石一入渠手不得再见 - 'Once this stone enters his hand, I shall never see it again' – indicating the stone's new owner (likely Zhao Peng) is not sharing it.
  • 筆想成圖彷佛在目 - 'In my mind's eye, I draw it, and it is as if before my eyes' – a poetic way of describing visualizing the stone in the mind.
  • 吾斋气秀尤不复泯矣 - 'The spirit of my study is especially bright and will not be obscured' – indicating the stone's effect on the atmosphere of the study.
  • 崇寜元年八月望 - A date: the 15th day of the 8th lunar month in the first year of the Chongning era (1102 CE), during the Song Dynasty.
  • 米芾书 - 'Written by Mi Fu' – the calligrapher and painter Mi Fu (1051–1107).
  • 吴仲圭 - Wu Zhonggui, a painter from Jiaxing, likely referring to Wu Zhen (1280–1354), a Yuan dynasty painter, though the name may be a variant.
  • 吴孟思书文后 - 'Wu Mengsi wrote the text after' – the calligrapher or scribe who wrote the commentary or inscription.
  • 携至吴兴燬于兵 - 'Carried to Wuxing and destroyed by the soldiers' – indicating that the painting or inscription was lost in a war or conflict.
  • 卫夫人 - 'Madam Wei' – likely referring to Wei Shuo (320–370), a famous calligrapher of the Jin Dynasty, or another woman of high status.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 官奴 - Refers to state-owned servants, often of low status. In the context of the passage, it is used to classify female entertainers as state property.
  • 周礼 - The *Rites of Zhou* (*Zhouli*), a foundational Confucian text that outlines the ideal bureaucratic and ceremonial system of the Zhou Dynasty.
  • 南唐 - The Southern Tang, a Southern Chinese kingdom during the Five Dynasties and Ten Kingdoms period (937–975 CE).
  • 研山 - A famous stone, possibly the 'Yanshan Stone' or 'Yan Mountain Stone,' a treasured object in Chinese literati culture, often used for calligraphy and painting.
  • 小翠峯 - 'Small emerald peak' – a poetic name for a rare and beautiful stone.
  • 米芾 - Mi Fu (1051–1107), a famous Song dynasty painter, calligrapher, and collector, known for his eccentricity and love of strange stones.
  • 吴仲圭 - Wu Zhonggui – possibly a misrendering of Wu Zhen (1280–1354), a Yuan dynasty painter known for his ink landscapes.
  • 吴孟思 - Wu Mengsi – likely a calligrapher or scribe who recorded the text.
  • 卫夫人 - Wei Shuo, a renowned calligrapher from the Jin Dynasty, known for her influence on the development of cursive script.
  • 崇寜元年 - The first year of the Chongning era (1102 CE), during the reign of Emperor Zhenzong of the Song Dynasty.
  • 地仙 - 'Earth immortal' – a term used in Daoist and literati culture to describe someone who has achieved a level of spiritual transcendence without ascending to heaven.

  • 能正書入妙 - 'Able to write regular script (正書) to the level of *miào* (excellence or mastery).'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 知公之书所以妙者无帖不习也 - Means 'knowing the reason why the public's writing is so wonderful is that he has practiced every model,' indicating comprehensive mastery of calligraphy.
  • 吾自幼好画马 - Means 'I have been fond of painting horses since childhood,' indicating a lifelong passion.
  • 自谓颇尽物之性 - Means 'I consider myself to have captured the essence of the subject,' reflecting the artist's self-assessment.
  • 友人郭祐之尝赠余诗云 - Means 'My friend Guo Youzhi once gave me a poem saying,' introducing a direct quotation.
  • 世人但解比龙眠 - Means 'People merely understand to compare with Longmian,' where 'Longmian' refers to the renowned painter Zhang Xuan.
  • 那知巳出曹韩上 - Means 'Who knows that it surpasses Cao and Han,' referring to the painters Cao Zhongda and Han Gan.
  • 曹韩固是过许使 - Means 'Cao and Han are indeed beyond Xu Shi,' another reference to Xu Shi, a famous painter.
  • 龙眠无恙当与之并驱耳 - Means 'If Longmian were still alive, he would be able to compete with them,' suggesting the artist's work is on par with historical masters.
  • 又尝见公题所画马云 - Means 'I have also seen the public inscribe his paintings of horses,' indicating the artist's practice of adding inscriptions to his works.
  • 无虑数十百轴 - Means 'Without counting, there are dozens or even hundreds of scrolls,' emphasizing the quantity of the artist's works.
  • 又闻公偶得米海岳书壮懐赋一卷 - Means 'I have also heard that the public once obtained a scroll of Mi Hailuo's *Zhuang Huai Fu*,' referring to the calligrapher Mi Fu.
  • 中阙数行 - Means 'With several lines missing in the middle,' indicating a defect in the scroll.
  • 因取刻本摹榻以补其阙 - Means 'He then took a printed version and copied it to fill the gap,' describing the process of restoration.
  • 凡易五七终不如意 - Means 'After changing it five or seven times, he was still not satisfied,' highlighting the artist's perfectionism.
  • 乃叹曰 - Means 'He then sighed and said,' introducing a direct quotation.
  • 今不逮古多矣 - Means 'Nowadays, we fall far behind the ancients,' expressing a sense of loss in artistic standards.
  • 遂以刻本完之 - Means 'He then completed it with the printed version,' describing the final step in the restoration.
  • 公之翰墨为国朝第一 - Means 'The public's calligraphy and painting are the best in the country,' praising the artist's skill.
  • 犹且服善如此 - Means 'Yet he still admits to his own shortcomings,' showing humility.
  • 近有一等人仅能防画如意 - Means 'Recently, there are some people who can only draw what they like,' criticizing those who lack versatility.
  • 便自夸大者于公宁不愧乎 - Means 'Such people would surely feel ashamed in comparison with the public,' contrasting the artist's humility with others' arrogance.
  • 金鼇山 - Likely a specific location or reference point.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 吾鄉于佩逺先生【演】题金鼇山诗曰 - 吾鄉 means 'my hometown', 于佩逺先生 refers to the person named Yu Pei Yuan, 【演】题 means 'composed' or 'wrote', and 金鼇山诗 refers to a poem about Jin Ao Mountain.
  • 金鼇之山金碧浮重寳坊居上头 - 金鼇之山 refers to Jin Ao Mountain, 金碧浮重 describes the mountain's golden and green appearance, 寳坊 refers to a temple or shrine, and 居上头 means 'situated at the top'.
  • 钟声夜度海门月树色逺揽丰山秋 - 钟声夜度 means 'the bell sounds echoing through the night', 海门月 refers to the moon over the sea gate, 树色逺揽 means 'the tree colors are far-reaching', and 丰山秋 refers to the autumn scenery of Feng Mountain.
  • 龙伯国人真妙手掣此巨灵镇江口 - 龙伯国人 refers to people from Longbo, 真妙手 means 'truly skilled hands', 掣此巨灵 means 'to wield this giant', and 镇江口 refers to the mouth of the river.
  • 丹丘逸士来跨之石洼为尊江当酒 - 丹丘逸士 refers to a reclusive scholar from Danqiu, 来跨之 means 'to ride it', 石洼为尊 means 'the stone depression as a seat', and 江当酒 means 'the river as wine'.
  • 黄须天子七寳鞭黄头渔郎櫂江船 - 黄须天子 refers to a king with yellow beard, 七寳鞭 means 'seven-jeweled whip', 黄头渔郎 refers to a fisherman with yellow hair, and 櫂江船 means 'rowing a boat on the river'.
  • 百年尘迹果何在芒砀云去山苍然 - 百年尘迹 means 'traces of a hundred years', 果何在 means 'where are they', 芒砀云去 refers to the clouds over Mangdang, and 山苍然 means 'the mountains are dark and gloomy'.
  • 歴试诸难固天造中兴开国何草草 - 歴试诸难 means 'having experienced many difficulties', 固天造 means 'truly created by heaven', 中兴开国 means 'reviving and founding a new dynasty', and 何草草 means 'how hasty'.
  • 腹心有疾日月昏英雄无声天地老 - 腹心有疾 means 'having illness in the heart', 日月昏 means 'the sun and moon are dim', 英雄无声 means 'heroes are silent', and 天地老 means 'heaven and earth grow old'.
  • 两宫不归汴水流此地空传帝子游 - 两宫不归 means 'the two palaces do not return', 汴水流 means 'the Bian River flows', 此地空传 means 'this place only transmits', and 帝子游 means 'the emperor's son's travels'.
  • 此诗至今脍炙人口山枕海属临海县章安镇 - 此诗至今脍炙人口 means 'this poem is still widely praised', 山枕海属 means 'the mountain rests by the sea', 临海县 refers to Linhai County, and 章安镇 refers to Zhang'an Town.
  • 初宋髙宗在潜邸日泰州人徐神翁云能知前来事 - 初宋髙宗在潜邸日 means 'at the beginning, Emperor Gaozong of Song was in his private residence', 泰州人 refers to a person from Taizhou, 徐神翁 refers to Xu Shenwang, and 云能知前来事 means 'said he could know past events'.
  • 群阉言于髙宗召至以賔礼接之一日献诗于帝曰 - 群阉言于髙宗 means 'the eunuchs spoke to Emperor Gaozong', 召至以賔礼接之 means 'he summoned him and received him with the courtesy of a guest', and 一日献诗于帝曰 means 'one day, he presented a poem to the emperor saying'.
  • 牡蛎滩头一艇横夕阳西去待潮生 - 牡蛎滩头 refers to the oyster beach, 一艇横 means 'a boat lying horizontally', 夕阳西去 means 'the sunset going west', and 待潮生 means 'waiting for the tide to rise'.
  • 与君不登临约同上金鼇背上行 - 与君不登临约 means 'without climbing together with you', and 同上金鼇背上行 means 'to walk together on the back of Jin Ao'.
  • 及两宫北狩匹马南渡建 - 及两宫北狩 means 'when the two palaces were taken north', 匹马南渡 means 'a single horse crossing south', and 建 means 'to establish'.

  • 李成 - Li Cheng, another renowned painter of the Five Dynasties and Song periods, known for his detailed and realistic landscapes.
  • 董巨 - Refers to both Dong Yuan and Juran, both influential in the development of Chinese landscape painting.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 山水中唯水口最难画 - Emphasizes the difficulty of depicting the water mouth in landscape painting.
  • 逺水无湾逺人无目 - Poetic way of saying that distant water appears straight and distant people appear without distinct features.
  • 水出髙源自上而下切不可断要取活流之源 - Suggests that when depicting water flowing from a high source, it should not be interrupted but should capture the essence of a living stream.
  • 山头要折搭转换山脉皆顺此活法也 - Indicates that mountain peaks should be depicted with dynamic changes and that the entire mountain range should follow this principle of vitality.
  • 众峯如相揖逊万树相从如大军领卒森然有不可犯之色此写真山之形也 - Describes how peaks should appear as if they are bowing to each other and trees as if they are following a leader, creating a majestic and inviolable image of a true mountain.
  • 山坡中可以置屋舎水中可置小艇从此有生气 - Suggests that adding houses on hillsides and small boats on water brings life to the scene.
  • 山腰用云气见得山埶髙不可测 - Recommends using clouds in the middle of the mountain to show its height and grandeur.
  • 画石之法最要形象不要石有三面或在上在左侧皆可为面临笔之际殆要取用 - Emphasizes the importance of the shape of stones in painting and suggests that stones can be depicted from various angles.
  • 山下有水潭谓之瀬画此甚有生意四边用树簇之 - Describes how to depict a waterfall at the foot of a mountain, which adds vitality to the scene, and suggests surrounding it with trees.
  • 画一窠一石当逸墨撇脱有士人家风才多便入画工之流矣 - Advises that painting a few rocks and trees with a free and spontaneous style reflects the taste of a scholar and not just a skilled artisan.
  • 或画山水一幅先立题目然后着笔若无题目便不成画 - Suggests that when painting a landscape, one should first establish a theme before starting to paint, as without a theme, the painting lacks coherence.
  • 更要记春夏秋冬景色春则万物发生夏则树木繁冗秋则万象肃杀冬则烟云黯淡天色糢糊 - Emphasizes the importance of capturing the different seasons in landscape painting, each with its own characteristics.
  • 李成画坡脚须要数层取其湿厚米元章论李光丞有后代儿孙昌盛果出为官者最多画亦有风水存焉 - Discusses the technique of depicting slopes in layers to achieve a sense of moisture and thickness, and mentions a historical example of how the success of descendants is reflected in their paintings.
  • 松树不见根喻君子在野杂树喻小人峥嵘之意 - Uses the metaphor of a pine tree without visible roots to represent a gentleman in the wild and mixed trees to represent the ruggedness of common people.
  • 夏山欲雨要带水笔山上有石小块堆在上谓之矾头用水笔晕开加淡螺青又是一般秀润画不过意思而已 - Describes how to depict a summer mountain before rain, using water brushes to create a misty effect and adding light blue-green to enhance the beauty of the scene.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 李成 - Famous painter from the Northern Song Dynasty known for his landscapes.
  • 米元章 - Famous calligrapher and painter from the Northern Song Dynasty, known for his theories on painting.
  • 李光丞 - Historical figure whose descendants' success is mentioned in the context of painting.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 山水中唯水口最难画 - The phrase '唯水口最难画' (only the water mouth is the hardest to paint) is a bit ambiguous, but it clearly emphasizes the difficulty of depicting the water mouth.
  • 众峯如相揖逊万树相从如大军领卒森然有不可犯之色此写真山之形也 - This long sentence describes the dynamic interaction between peaks and trees, creating a majestic and inviolable image of a true mountain.
  • 山腰用云气见得山埶髙不可测 - The phrase '见得山埶髙不可测' (shows the height of the mountain is immeasurable) is a poetic way of emphasizing the grandeur of the mountain.
  • 画一窠一石当逸墨撇脱有士人家风才多便入画工之流矣 - The phrase '有士人家风' (reflects the taste of a scholar) is a bit ambiguous but suggests a refined and cultured style.
  • 或画山水一幅先立题目然后着笔若无题目便不成画 - The phrase '若无题目便不成画' (if there is no theme, the painting is not complete) emphasizes the importance of having a clear theme in landscape painting.
  • 更要记春夏秋冬景色春则万物发生夏则树木繁冗秋则万象肃杀冬则烟云黯淡天色糢糊 - The phrase '更要记春夏秋冬景色' (more importantly, remember the scenes of the four seasons) introduces the importance of capturing the seasonal changes in landscape painting.
  • 李成画坡脚须要数层取其湿厚米元章论李光丞有后代儿孙昌盛果出为官者最多画亦有风水存焉 - The phrase '画亦有风水存焉' (painting also has a sense of feng shui) is a bit ambiguous but suggests that the success of descendants is reflected in their paintings.

  • 制衣袖广狭脩短与臂同 - Means that the sleeves of their clothes were wide, narrow, long, or short, matching the length of their arms.
  • 衣幅长不过膝 - Indicates that the length of the clothing did not exceed the knees.
  • 袴如袖裙如衣 - Describes trousers that were like sleeves and skirts that were like dresses.
  • 总名曰草裙草袴 - Refers to the overall style of clothing being called 'grass skirts' and 'grass trousers.'
  • 固脰以兽皮曰护项 - Means that they fastened their necks with animal skins, called 'protective collars.'
  • 束要以帛两端悬凥后若尾 - Describes tying the waist with silk, with the ends hanging down behind like a tail.
  • 无间晴雨被氊毯状 - Indicates that they wore felt blankets regardless of whether it was sunny or rainy.
  • 絶犬 - Means that they were entirely like dogs.
  • 按邕管杂记溪蛮丛笑等书所载五溪之蛮尽槃瓠种属 - Refers to the fact that the various tribes along the Five Rivers were all descendants of the Pangu (a legendary figure).
  • 曰猫曰徭曰曰犵狑曰犵狫字皆从犬 - Lists the names of these tribes, all of which contain the character for 'dog,' indicating their canine-like characteristics.
  • 则谚所谓苖犬者信然 - Confirms the proverb that these tribes were indeed like dogs.
  • 其锣若 - Indicates that the gongs were used in a specific way, possibly for signaling or coordination.

  • 驿使 - Refers to messengers or couriers in ancient China, indicating a well-organized communication system.

  • 槃瓠 - A legendary figure in Chinese mythology, often associated with the origin of certain ethnic groups.
  • 五溪之蛮 - Refers to the various tribes living along the Five Rivers in southern China.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 驿使交道中不絶丞相兵少防无所出以旺札勒来守之 - This sentence describes the continuous flow of messengers and the Chancellor's inability to defend due to a lack of soldiers, followed by the arrival of Wang Zale to guard the area.
  • 旺札勒取道自杭以兵刼丞相陞本省参知政事 - This sentence describes Wang Zale's journey from Hangzhou with his troops and the subsequent promotion of the Chancellor.
  • 填募民入粟空名告身予之即拜添设左丞 - This sentence describes the practice of recruiting civilians to provide grain and granting them official documents, followed by the appointment of Wang Zale as an additional Left Chancellor.
  • 所统苖防洞猺达拉哈等无尺籍伍符无统属相谓曰阿哥曰麻线至称主将亦然 - This sentence describes the various groups under Wang Zale's command, their lack of official records, and their conversation about the main commander.
  • 喜着斑斓衣制衣袖广狭脩短与臂同衣幅长不过膝袴如袖裙如衣总名曰草裙草袴固脰以兽皮曰护项束要以帛两端悬凥后若尾无间晴雨被氊毯状絶犬 - This sentence describes the distinctive clothing and attire of these groups, emphasizing their canine-like characteristics.
  • 按邕管杂记溪蛮丛笑等书所载五溪之蛮尽槃瓠种属曰猫曰徭曰曰犵狑曰犵狫字皆从犬则谚所谓苖犬者信然 - This sentence references historical and literary sources to confirm the canine-like characteristics of these tribes.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 忍言官庾尚有粟四十万余籍为己有 - Indicates that a certain official, possibly named '忍言官庾尚,' had stored forty thousand or more units of grain, which he claimed as his own.
  • 越五十日平江兵破淀湖栅 - Suggests that after fifty days, the forces from Pingjiang broke through the defenses at Dianhu. '越' means 'after' or 'passed,' indicating a passage of time.
  • 苖夜遁去 - Describes a retreat or escape by the enemy forces during the night. '遁' means to flee or escape.
  • 秋平江兵入杭 - Indicates that in autumn, the Pingjiang forces entered Hangzhou.
  • 苗将吴大旺败 - States that the enemy general Wu Dawang was defeated.
  • 旺札勒自嘉兴来驻兵城中菜市桥外未即进 - Suggests that Wang Zale came from Jiaxing and stationed his troops outside the Cai Shiqiao bridge in the city but did not immediately advance. '未即进' means 'did not immediately advance.'
  • 嘉兴仅保孤城城之外悉遭兵燹 - Suggests that only the isolated city of Jiaxing remained intact, while the surrounding areas were completely devastated by war. '兵燹' means destruction caused by war.
  • 有穷目力所至无寸草尺木处 - Describes a scene where there was no vegetation within the range of one's sight. '穷目力' means to look as far as one's eyes can see.
  • 旺札勒虽阳浮尊事丞相生杀予夺于己是决丞相仅得署成案然浙江之南则行御史台总督官穆尔古蘓建徳路则逹噜噶齐衮都罗丹各自为守苗不敢犯其境 - Indicates that although Wang Zale pretended to show respect to the Chancellor, he made all decisions regarding life and death. The Chancellor could only approve the final decisions. However, in the southern part of Zhejiang, the Inspector General Muergu Si and in Jianshi Road, the Governor Dunuqi Gundi each maintained their own defenses, and the enemy dared not invade their territories.
  • 旺札勒之威令仅行于杭州嘉兴两郡而已 - Suggests that Wang Zale's authority and commands were limited to the two cities of Hangzhou and Jiaxing.
  • 筑营徳胜堰周围三四里子女玉帛皆在焉且以为郿坞计用法刻深任埶立威而邓子文金希 - Describes the construction of a camp at De Sheng Yan, which was surrounded by several miles. Inside the camp were women, jade, and silk. Wang Zale planned to use this as a stronghold and enforced strict laws to establish his authority. However, the names '邓子文' (Deng Ziwén) and '金希' (Jin Xi) are unclear and may require further research.

  • 我适拾得但不审果是汝物否 - Phrase meaning 'I just picked it up, but I'm not sure if it's yours.'
  • 方再三磨问颗数装束实是 - Phrase meaning 'After repeatedly asking about the number of items and their packaging, it turned out to be true.'
  • 遂同造主人 - Phrase meaning 'So we went together to the owner.'
  • 主人感谢欲赠以礼辞不受 - Phrase meaning 'The owner thanked us and wanted to give us a gift, but we refused.'
  • 既而主人怒此婢遣嫁业梳剃者所居去渡所咫尺间期嵗 - Phrase meaning 'Later, the owner was angry and sent the maid to be married to a barber who lived very close to the ferry, within a year.'
  • 少年与同行二十有八人将过渡道遇一妇人拜且谢视之乃失环女也因告其故于夫屈留午饭余人先登舟俄风涛大作皆鱼腹 - Phrase meaning 'The young man and his twenty-eight companions were about to cross the river when they met a woman who bowed and thanked them. Upon seeing her, they recognized her as the woman who had lost her ring. They told her husband the story, and he invited them to stay for lunch. The others boarded the boat first, but soon a storm arose, and all were drowned.'
  • 盖少年能救人一命而造物者亦救其一命以荅之后少年以夀终 - Phrase meaning 'It was because the young man saved someone's life that the creator also saved his life as a reward. Later, the young man lived a long life.'
  • 渡在温之瑞安 - Phrase meaning 'The ferry is located in Ruian, Wenzhou.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 汉子 - Term meaning 'a low-ranking man' or 'a commoner.'
  • 今人谓贱丈夫曰汉子 - Phrase meaning 'Nowadays, people call a low-ranking man 'hànzi.'
  • 北齐魏恺自散骑常侍迁青州长史固辞文宣帝大怒曰何物汉子与官不就 - Phrase meaning 'During the Northern Qi dynasty, Wei Kai, who was a scion of the imperial family, refused to accept the position of Governor of Qingzhou and was appointed as a Palace Attendant. Emperor Wenxuan was furious and said, 'What kind of hànzi refuses an official position?'
  • 叚成式庐陵官下记韦令去西蜀时彭州刺史被县令宻论诉韦前期勘知屈刺史诣府陈谢及回日诸县令悉逺迎所诉者为首大言曰使君今日可谓朱研益丹矣刺史笑曰则公便是研朱汉子也 - Phrase meaning 'Duan Chengshi, the official of Luzhou, recorded that Wei Ling, the magistrate of Xishu, was accused by the county magistrate. Wei Ling investigated and found that the accusation was false. He went to the governor's office to apologize. When he returned, all the county magistrates came to greet him. The accuser, who was the leader, said, 'Today, Sir, you have truly made red even redder.' The governor laughed and said, 'Then you are the hànzi who makes red even redder.'
  • 长年 - Term meaning 'an old boatman.'
  • 吾乡称舟人之老者曰长老长上声盖唐已有之矣 - Phrase meaning 'In my hometown, we call an old boatman 'longzhang'. This term has been used since the Tang dynasty.'
  • 古今诗话谓川峡以篙手为三老乃推一船之最尊者言之耳 - Phrase meaning 'According to the 'Poetic Notes of Ancient and Modern Times,' in the Sichuan and Chongqing region, the 'three elders' refer to the most respected person on the boat.'
  • 因思海舶中以司柁曰大翁是亦长老三老之意 - Phrase meaning 'Therefore, in sea vessels, the person in charge of the rudder is called 'dàwēng,' which also carries the meaning of 'longzhang' and 'three elders.'

Proper Nouns

  • 渡在温之瑞安 - Specific location meaning 'The ferry is located in Ruian, Wenzhou.'
  • 北齐魏恺 - Historical figure meaning 'Wei Kai during the Northern Qi dynasty.'
  • 叚成式 - Historical figure meaning 'Duan Chengshi, the official of Luzhou.'
  • 杜工部 - Historical figure meaning 'Du Fu, the poet.'
  • 古今诗话 - Historical text meaning 'Poetic Notes of Ancient and Modern Times.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 龍見嘉兴 - Literal translation; in Chinese, dragons often symbolize auspicious or supernatural events, but here it seems to describe a natural phenomenon.
  • 白龍掛盲風怪雨 - Describes a dramatic weather event, possibly a tornado or hurricane, metaphorically attributed to a dragon.
  • 天闇黒若深夜然 - Emphasizes the severity of the weather.
  • 壞民居五百余所 - Indicates the extent of the damage; '五百余所' (more than 500) is an approximate count, emphasizing the scale.
  • 大木盡防木自半空墜下悉折為二 - Describes the force of the wind, using parallel structure to emphasize the destruction.
  • 雜以萬瓦亂飛 - Further illustrates the chaos; '萬瓦' (countless tiles) and '亂飛' (flying about) create a sense of chaos.
  • 溪水直立 - Metaphorical description of the water's turbulence.
  • 人皆呌號奔走不暇顧妻子 - Conveys the panic and urgency; '呌號奔走' (screaming and running) and '不暇顧妻子' (too busy to look after their wives and children) emphasize the panic.
  • 龍由馬橋歴城北北麗橋望太湖而去 - Provides a geographical path; the long sentence describes the dragon's path, using a series of locations to create a geographical narrative.
  • 時方在家家去城可三里許 - Gives the narrator's location; '時方在家' (at the time, I was at home) and '家去城可三里許' (about three miles from the city) provide the narrator's perspective.
  • 如聞萬屋齊壓 - Describes the noise; '如聞萬屋齊壓' (it sounded like ten thousand houses were collapsing together) uses hyperbole to describe the noise.
  • 經一二時方乃開霽 - Indicates the duration of the storm; '經一二時方乃開霽' (after one or two hours, the sky cleared) indicates the duration of the storm.
  • 不一年為戰鬬之地 - Suggests the aftermath; '不一年為戰鬬之地' (within a year, it became a battlefield) suggests the aftermath.
  • 凡龍所過處荊棘寒煙衰草野燐視昔時之繁華如一夢也 - Describes the desolation; '凡龍所過處荊棘寒煙衰草野燐視昔時之繁華如一夢也' (wherever the dragon passed, there were thorns, cold smoke, withered grass, and wild fires, making the former prosperity seem like a dream) describes the desolation.
  • 星入月 - Literal description of a celestial event.
  • 忽見一星大如桮碗色白而防青尾長四五丈光焰燭天 - Vividly describes the star; '忽見一星大如桮碗色白而防青尾長四五丈光焰燭天' (suddenly, I saw a star as large as a cup and bowl, white and slightly blue, with a tail four or five zhang long, its light illuminating the sky) vividly describes the star.
  • 戞然有聲由東北方飛入月中而止 - Describes the star's movement; '戞然有聲由東北方飛入月中而止' (it made a clanging sound and flew into the moon from the northeast, stopping there) describes the star's movement.
  • 此時月如仰瓦正乗之無偏倚 - Describes the moon's position; '此時月如仰瓦正乗之無偏倚' (at this time, the moon was like an inverted tile, perfectly centered) describes the moon's position.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 不可住径唤渡过建康 - Colloquial expression meaning 'cannot stop and immediately call for a ferry to cross to Jiankang.'
  • 古有此否 - Question meaning 'Is there any record of this in ancient times?'
  • 邹云汉献帝时一次星入月 - Specific historical event: 'Zou Yun said that during Emperor Xian's reign, there was once a star entering the moon.'
  • 今再见也 - Statement meaning 'now it is seen again.'
  • 刘倬举兵僇孝姑姑反戈一城狼狈仅以身免继此兵祸未泯也 - Complex sentence describing a military event: 'Liu Zhuo raised an army to kill his aunt, who turned against him, leaving the city in disarray; he barely escaped with his life, and the subsequent military disaster has not yet ended.'
  • 据此説则松江之祸亦非偶然 - Conclusion based on the previous statement: 'According to this account, the disaster in Songjiang was also not accidental.'
  • 松江自丙申二月十八日军乱越三日苗来尅复首尾两月之间焚杀掳掠十里之城悉化瓦砾之区视他郡尤可畏是则星入月不知此时在于何所分野顾乃松江独应其兆与 - Detailed description of the disaster in Songjiang: 'Since the military uprising in Songjiang on the 18th day of the second month of Bing Shen, over three days, Miao came to suppress it. In the two months that followed, the ten-mile city was completely destroyed by fire, slaughter, and looting, turning into a wasteland. Compared to other counties, it is even more terrifying. The star entering the moon is unknown at what time and in which region it occurred, but Songjiang alone seems to have responded to this omen.'
  • 军中礼士 - Title or theme: 'Etiquette for soldiers.'
  • 浙省参政董公搏霄字【孟起】以名行当至正癸巳之间总兵戌昱岭独松千秋三闗日号令严肃民赖以安及尅复诸郡不杀掳不抄掠其御将帅也凛然不可犯而四方之士归之者礼遇勤至尊酒在前起立捧觞既恭且和然后取其所长而任之若董公者可谓得待士之体矣 - Detailed description of a political figure: 'Zheng Sheng, the Governor of Zhejiang, named Dong Bo Xiao, with the courtesy name Mengqi, during the period of Zhizheng Gui Si, was the overall commander of the military garrison at Yuling, Dusu, Qianqiu, and San Pass. He issued strict orders, and the people relied on him for safety. When he recaptured various counties, he did not kill or loot, and his discipline was so strict that no one dared to defy him. Scholars from all over came to him, and he treated them with great courtesy, standing up to pour wine when it was presented, showing both respect and harmony. He then took their strengths and appointed them accordingly. If one were to follow Dong Gong's example, it could be said that he truly understood how to treat scholars.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '不可住径唤渡过建康' is elliptical and grammatically compact, requiring expansion for clarity.
  • The question '古有此否' is straightforward but requires context to understand its significance.
  • The sentence '邹云汉献帝时一次星入月今再见也' is a historical reference that needs to be expanded to provide context.
  • The sentence '刘倬举兵僇孝姑姑反戈一城狼狈仅以身免继此兵祸未泯也' is complex and requires careful parsing to convey the sequence of events.
  • The sentence '据此説则松江之祸亦非偶然' is a conclusion based on the previous statement, requiring clear logical connection.
  • The sentence '松江自丙申二月十八日军乱越三日苗来尅复首尾两月之间焚杀掳掠十里之城悉化瓦砾之区视他郡尤可畏是则星入月不知此时在于何所分野顾乃松江独应其兆与' is a detailed description of the disaster, requiring careful segmentation to maintain clarity.
  • The title '军中礼士' is straightforward but requires context to understand its significance.
  • The sentence '浙省参政董公搏霄字【孟起】以名行当至正癸巳之间总兵戌昱岭独松千秋三闗日号令严肃民赖以安及尅复诸郡不杀掳不抄掠其御将帅也凛然不可犯而四方之士归之者礼遇勤至尊酒在前起立捧觞既恭且和然后取其所长而任之若董公者可谓得待士之体矣' is a detailed description of a political figure, requiring careful parsing to convey the sequence of actions and qualities.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 健司马之雄放 - Refers to the strength and boldness of the Wei (Cao Wei) period, particularly associated with figures like Cao Cao or his descendants.
  • 三国之文孔明之二表 - Refers to the famous 'Memorial to the Emperor' (出师表) written by Zhuge Liang (孔明) during the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 建安诸子之数书 - Refers to the works of the Jian'an (建安) era writers, known for their literary achievements.
  • 西晋之文渊明归去来辞 - Refers to Tao Yuanming's (陶渊明) famous work 'Returning to the Fields' (归去来兮辞).
  • 李令伯陈情表 - Refers to Li Mi's (李密) 'Memorial of Sincerity' (陈情表), a famous piece of prose.
  • 王逸少兰亭叙 - Refers to Wang Xizhi's (王羲之) 'Preface to the Poems Composed at the Orchid Pavilion' (兰亭序), a renowned calligraphy and literary work.
  • 刻削 - Refers to precise and meticulous writing.

  • 八珍 - Refers to the eight delicacies of ancient Chinese cuisine.
  • 古文以辨而 - Refers to the essence of classical Chinese writing, emphasizing clarity and distinction.

  • 建安诸子 - The Jian'an era writers, including Cao Cao, Cao Pi, and Cao Zhi, who were influential in the development of Chinese literature.
  • 西晋 - The Western Jin dynasty, a period known for its literary achievements, including Tao Yuanming.
  • 渊明 - Tao Yuanming, a poet and philosopher known for his reclusive lifestyle and literary works.
  • 李令伯 - Li Mi, a writer and official during the Jin dynasty, known for his 'Memorial of Sincerity.'
  • 王逸少 - Wang Xizhi, a calligrapher and writer known for his 'Preface to the Poems Composed at the Orchid Pavilion.'
  • 韩 - Han Yu, a Tang dynasty writer and philosopher, known for his 'Discourse on Teachers' (师说).
  • 欧 - Ouyang Xiu, a Song dynasty writer and historian, known for his elegant and refined prose.
  • 苏 - Su Shi, a Song dynasty writer and poet, known for his versatile and expressive prose.

  • 司马相如 - Sima Xiangru, a Han dynasty writer known for his strict adherence to Confucian norms.
  • 元次山 - Yuan Zhi, a Tang dynasty writer known for his precise and meticulous prose.
  • 樊宗师 - Fan Zongshi, a Tang dynasty writer known for his laborious and meticulous writing.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '常熟州防松江府印造官号给散吏兵佩带以防奸伪' is a long clause that must be read as a single unit, describing the purpose and distribution of the official numbers.
  • The sentence '号之制作画为圆圏绕圏皆火焰圏之内一府字以府印印府字圏之外四角府官花押' is a complex sentence that describes the design of the official number, which must be read as a single, detailed description.
  • The sentence '民间謡曰满城都是火府官四散躱城里无一人红军府上坐不二月城破悉如所言' is a rhyme that must be read as a single unit, describing the predicted outcome of the situation.
  • The sentence '杭州赤山之隂曰箫箕泉黄大痴所尝结庐处其徒弟沈生狎近侧一女道姑同门有欲白之于师沈惧引厨刀自割其埶防死众救得活而疮口流血经月余不合偶问诸阉奴教以煆所割埶捣粉酒服如其言不数日而愈' describes a series of events, which must be read as a single, continuous narrative.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 食品有名 - Straightforward title meaning 'Famous Foods,' introducing the topic of notable dishes.

  • 河豚鱼也无鳞颊常怒气满腹形殊弗雅然味极佳 - Detailed description of the pufferfish: it lacks scales, has a puffed-up appearance, and is not aesthetically pleasing, but its taste is exquisite.
  • 煑治不精则能杀人 - Warns that improper preparation of the pufferfish can be fatal.
  • 据其味真是消得一死 - Hyperbolic expression meaning that the taste is so good it’s worth dying for.
  • 浙西惟江隂人尤珍之 - Indicates that people in Jiangyin, Zhejiang, particularly value the pufferfish.
  • 每春首初出时必用羞祭品毕然后作羮而邻里间互相餽送以为礼 - Describes a ritual where the pufferfish is prepared as a stew after a sacrificial offering, and neighbors exchange it as a gift.
  • 腹中之膟曰西施乳 - The pufferfish’s internal organ is compared to 'West Beauty's Milk,' a reference to a legendary beauty.
  • 夫西施一美妇也岂乳亦异于人耶 - Questions whether the pufferfish’s organ is truly different from human milk, given that it is named after a famous beauty.
  • 顾千载而下乃使人道之不置如此则夫差之亡国非偶然矣 - Reflects on the historical fate of King Fuchai of Wu, suggesting that his downfall was not coincidental.
  • 若鲚鱼子名螳螂子 - Describes the roe of the river catfish, which is called 'Grasshopper’s Eggs' due to its resemblance to grasshopper eggs.
  • 松江之上海杭州之海寜人皆喜食蟛蜞螯名曰鹦哥嘴 - Describes the preference of people in Songjiang, Shanghai, and Hai Ning, Hangzhou, for the claws of the Chinese mitten crab, which are called 'Parrot’s Beaks' due to their red color.
  • 二物象形而云又非西施乳之比矣 - Compares the two foods (pufferfish and crab claws) to 'West Beauty's Milk,' noting that they are different in nature.
  • 按编鱼部引博雅云鯸防【盈之反】鲀也背青腹白触物即怒其肝杀人正今人名为河豚者也然则豚当为鲀 - Cites a classical source to explain that the pufferfish is also known as 'Tun,' and its liver is poisonous.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 乗夜回櫂 - Sailed back at night; *乗夜* = 'at night,' *回櫂* = 'to turn the oar back' or 'to sail back.'
  • 大風覆舟 - A great wind capsized the boat; *覆舟* = 'to capsize a boat.'
  • 行李皆渰溺無余 - All belongings were submerged and lost without remainder; *渰溺* = 'to drown' or 'to submerge,' *無余* = 'without remainder' or 'all gone.'
  • 隂府辯詞 - Argument before the Underworld Tribunal or a Case Before the Netherworld Court; *隂府* = 'Underworld' or 'Netherworld,' *辯詞* = 'argument' or 'defense.'
  • 側室刁氏有娠 - The concubine Diao gave birth; *側室* = 'concubine' or 'secondary wife,' *有娠* = 'was pregnant.'
  • 棰挞苦楚 - Beaten with cruel punishment; *棰挞* = 'to beat or strike with a rod,' *苦楚* = 'pain' or 'suffering.'
  • 自經與溺以省覺不得死 - She attempted suicide by hanging and drowning, but upon coming to her senses, could not die; *自經* = 'to hang oneself,' *與溺* = 'and drowning,' *省覺* = 'to become aware' or 'to regain consciousness,' *不得死* = 'could not die.'
  • 竊自念曰 - She secretly thought to herself; *竊* = 'secretly' or 'privately,' *自念* = 'thought to oneself.'
  • 我苦就蓐亦必死耳 - If I suffer childbirth, I will certainly die; *就蓐* = 'to give birth' or 'to lie in bed for childbirth.'
  • 等死何自求早死之為幸 - What difference is it whether I die now or later? To die early is a blessing; *等死* = 'to die equally' or 'equally doomed to die,' *何自求早死之為幸* = 'why should I seek to die early as a blessing?'
  • 多食海蛰與冷水 - Ate a lot of jellyfish and drank cold water; *海蛰* = 'sea jelly' or 'jellyfish,' *冷水* = 'cold water.'
  • 胎既落血上充心 - After the fetus fell, blood surged to the heart; *胎* = 'fetus,' *血上充心* = 'blood surged upward to the heart.'
  • 形骸枯槁無復生理 - Her body became emaciated and showed no signs of life; *形骸* = 'body' or 'form,' *枯槁* = 'withered' or 'emaciated,' *無復生理* = 'no longer had vital energy.'
  • 家人益厭之 - The family grew increasingly repulsed by her; *益* = 'more' or 'increasingly,' *厭之* = 'to hate/loathe her.'
  • 扣寝室戸 - Knocked on the bedroom door; *扣* = 'to knock,' *寝室* = 'bedroom,' *戸* = 'door.'
  • 願見舅姑具告所然 - I wish to see my in-laws and fully explain what happened; *願見* = 'wish to see,' *舅姑* = 'in-laws' (husband's parents), *具告所然* = 'fully explain what happened.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 妾为亡婢诉寃 - I was a servant who had died and was appealing for justice.
  • 摄至隂府即今岳祠也 - Taken to the underworld, which is now known as the Yue Temple.
  • 命妾与婢对词 - Ordered me to confront the servant in court.
  • 妾以汝懐孕时打骂则或有之 - I admit that during your pregnancy, I might have scolded you.
  • 然未尝令汝吞药损堕 - But I never made you take medicine that could harm the fetus.
  • 婢仇执甚坚 - The servant's accusation was very strong.
  • 妾不得白 - I could not prove my innocence.
  • 遂招承枷禁幽圄中 - So I was forced to confess and was locked up in a dark prison.
  • 故不馁 - So I did not starve.
  • 但觉怠倦故勉强至此 - I only feel tired, so I am struggling to get here.
  • 舅姑曰汝既被禁何自得释 - My husband and mother-in-law said, 'Since you were imprisoned, how did you get released?'
  • 曰防上帝有赦故也 - I said, 'Because the Heavenly Emperor had a pardon.'
  • 急呼小郎妻问之曰适间儿子惊啼云梦见乃父击其首 - They quickly called the younger son's wife to ask, 'Just now, our son was startled and cried, saying he dreamed that your father hit his head.'
  • 志怪古或多然漫书于此以为世之妬妇劝诗法 - This strange story is recorded here as a warning to women who are jealous and prone to gossip.

  • 隂府 - Refers to the underworld in Chinese mythology.

  • 舅姑 - Refers to husband and mother-in-law.
  • 提控 - Refers to a magistrate or local official.

  • 妬妇劝诗法 - Refers to a moral lesson about jealousy and gossip.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This long sentence must be read as a single narrative sequence: 'Xin Zhi then asked for details, and I, a deceased servant, was appealing for justice, taken to the underworld (now known as the Yue Temple), and ordered to confront the servant in court.'
  • This long sequence must be read as a single narrative sequence: 'I admit that during your pregnancy, I might have scolded you, but I never made you take medicine that could harm the fetus. The servant's accusation was very strong, and I could not prove my innocence, so I was forced to confess and was locked up in a dark prison. Each day, my younger brother brought me food like bread, cakes, and porridge, so I did not starve. Now, I am returned and allowed to enter the house to play with my child, but when I lightly hit him, he cries. So I was pushed onto the stove, and upon waking, I found myself there. I only feel tired, so I am struggling to get here. My husband and mother-in-law said, 'Since you were imprisoned, how did you get released?' I said, 'Because the Heavenly Emperor had a pardon.' They quickly called the younger son's wife to ask, 'Just now, our son was startled and cried, saying he dreamed that your father hit his head.' The younger son, who is the second son of the magistrate, was previously killed by his sister-in-law's husband, Jin Keda. This woman is still healthy and lives with her husband in the western suburbs of the city, and they have had two more children. This strange story is recorded here as a warning to women who are jealous and prone to gossip.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 视民如赤子 - Classical idiom meaning 'to regard the people as one's own children,' emphasizing benevolence and care.
  • 养士若兄弟 - Classical idiom meaning 'to treat scholars and officials like brothers,' emphasizing close bonds and mutual support.
  • 练万众以身 - Classical idiom meaning 'to train the masses by example,' emphasizing personal leadership and role modeling.
  • 六合之内为一统 - Classical idiom meaning 'to unify the entire world,' emphasizing the goal of a unified empire.
  • 南连赵宋北接回纥东夏西夷悉称臣佐 - Unclear phrase, possibly meaning 'to connect to the south with Zhao and Song, to the north with the Uighurs, to the east with the Eastern Xia, and to the west with the Western Xia, all of whom submit and assist.'
  • 念我单于国千载百世以来未之有也 - Unclear phrase, possibly meaning 'to think of my Chanyu state, which has never been seen in a thousand years and a hundred generations.'
  • 然而任大守重治平犹惧有阙 - Unclear phrase, possibly meaning 'however, with great responsibility and heavy governance, I still fear there may be shortcomings.'
  • 且夫刳舟剡楫将欲济江河也聘贤选佐将以安天下也 - Unclear phrase, possibly meaning 'and so, to carve boats and sharpen paddles to cross the rivers and lakes, to recruit virtuous people and select assistants to stabilize the world.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 单于 - Refers to the leader of the Xiongnu, a nomadic confederation that was a major power in northern China during the Han dynasty.
  • 赵宋 - Refers to the states of Zhao and Song, which were part of the Warring States period and later became part of the Han dynasty.
  • 回纥 - Refers to the Uighurs, a Turkic people who established a powerful kingdom in Central Asia.
  • 东夏 - Refers to the Eastern Xia, a state founded by the Tangut people in the 11th century.
  • 西夷 - Refers to the Western Xia, another state founded by the Tangut people in the 11th century.
  • 三九之位 - Refers to high-ranking officials or positions in the government, often associated with the Three Excellencies (三公) and the Nine Ministers (九卿).

Proper Nouns

  • 单于 - The leader of the Xiongnu.
  • 赵宋 - States of Zhao and Song.
  • 回纥 - The Uighurs.
  • 东夏 - The Eastern Xia.
  • 西夷 - The Western Xia.
  • 丘师先生 - A virtuous and knowledgeable advisor.

  • 仙歩 - Refers to the steps of a sage or a highly respected person, often used to show respect.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 不才 - A humble self-reference meaning 'unworthy' or 'unskilled,' often used by scholars to downplay their abilities.
  • 海上居民 - Literally 'residents of the sea,' likely referring to people living near the coast or in maritime regions.
  • 心皆恍惚 - Means 'all hearts are confused or disoriented,' suggesting a state of uncertainty or bewilderment.
  • 處機自念 - Means 'reflecting on one's situation,' with '處機' implying a moment of decision or reflection.
  • 謀生太拙 - Means 'too clumsy at making a living,' indicating a lack of success in worldly pursuits.
  • 學道無成 - Means 'failed to achieve success in the Dao,' referring to the pursuit of spiritual or philosophical enlightenment.
  • 辛苦萬端 - Means 'endless hardships,' emphasizing the difficulties faced.
  • 老而不死 - A literal translation, but it may carry a negative connotation, implying that the speaker feels they are not yet ready to die.
  • 名雖播於諸國 - Means 'though fame has spread among various countries,' indicating a degree of recognition.
  • 道不加於众人 - Means 'the Dao has not benefited the common people,' suggesting a sense of failure in spreading wisdom.
  • 内顧自傷 - Means 'looking inward and feeling self-pity,' indicating a moment of self-reflection and sorrow.
  • 情誰測 - Means 'who can understand my feelings,' expressing a sense of isolation or misunderstanding.
  • 前者南京及宋國屢召不從 - Means 'before, when Nanjing and Song repeatedly invited me, I did not comply,' indicating a history of rejecting invitations.
  • 今者龍庭一呼即至 - Means 'now, when the Dragon Court calls, I come immediately,' suggesting a change in attitude or circumstances.
  • 伏聞皇帝天賜勇智 - Means 'I have heard that the Emperor is endowed with courage and wisdom by heaven,' indicating a belief in the Emperor's divine qualities.
  • 華夷率服 - Means 'the Chinese and foreigners all submit,' indicating widespread obedience.
  • 便欲投山竄海 - Means 'I wish to hide in the mountains and escape to the sea,' suggesting a desire to retreat from society.
  • 不忍相違 - Means 'cannot bear to disobey,' indicating a sense of duty or obligation.
  • 圖其一見 - Means 'to see him once,' indicating a desire for a meeting.
  • 車駕只在桓撫之北 - Means 'the Emperor's carriage is only north of Huanfu,' suggesting the Emperor's location.
  • 燕京 - Refers to the capital city, likely referring to modern-day Beijing.
  • 假之皇帝所 - Means 'if I were to approach the Emperor,' indicating a hypothetical scenario.
  • 軍國之事非己所能 - Means 'military and state affairs are beyond my ability,' suggesting a lack of expertise in governance.
  • 悉為難事 - Means 'all are difficult matters,' emphasizing the challenges faced.
  • 遂與宣差劉仲祿商議 - Means 'and then discussed with Xuancha Liu Zhonglu,' indicating a consultation with a messenger.
  • 先令人前去奏知 - Means 'first send someone to report beforehand,' indicating a preliminary step.
  • 劉仲祿不從故不 - Means 'Liu Zhonglu did not agree, so I did not,' indicating a decision based on the messenger's advice.

  • 南京 - Refers to Nanjing, a major city in China, likely referring to the Southern Song capital.
  • 宋國 - Refers to the Song Dynasty, a historical period in China.
  • 龍庭 - Refers to the Dragon Court, a metaphorical term for the imperial court.
  • 桓撫之北 - Refers to a specific location, likely a place name in the context of the narrative.
  • 燕京 - Refers to the capital city, likely referring to modern-day Beijing.
  • 宣差劉仲祿 - Refers to a messenger named Liu Zhonglu, likely a historical figure.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 免自纳奏帖 - Formal expression indicating that the author has submitted a petition or memorial.
  • 念处机肯来归命 - Phrase expressing gratitude for the submission and acknowledgment of the petitioner's willingness to submit.
  • 详其可否 - Request to carefully consider the matter.
  • 兼同时四人出家三人得道 - Statement about the outcomes of four people who became monks and three who attained enlightenment.
  • 惟处机虚得其名 - Critique suggesting that the petitioner has only gained a reputation without substance.
  • 圣裁 - Term indicating the emperor's decision or judgment.
  • 应召而来者具悉 - Statement acknowledging that the emperor is aware of those who have responded to the call.
  • 时与愿适天不人违 - Phrase indicating that the timing and circumstances were favorable, and that the petitioner's actions were in harmony with the will of heaven.
  • 两朝屡召而弗行 - Statement that the petitioner had been summoned by two emperors but did not respond.
  • 单使一邀而肯起 - Statement that the petitioner agreed to come after being invited by a single envoy.
  • 谓朕天启所以身归不辞暴露于风霜 - Statement that the emperor believes it was heaven's will for the petitioner to return, despite enduring harsh conditions.
  • 书章来上喜慰何言 - Statement that the emperor is pleased and relieved upon receiving the petitioner's submission.
  • 军国之事非朕所期 - Statement that the emperor does not expect the petitioner to be involved in military or state affairs.
  • 朕以彼酋不逊我伐用张单旅试临边陲 - Statement that the emperor has ordered a military expedition against a rebellious leader.
  • 底定来从去背实力率之 - Statement that the emperor's forces have pacified the region and the people have followed or defected.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 青吉斯皇帝 - Refers to Genghis Khan, the founder of the Mongol Empire.
  • 真人丘师省 - Refers to Qiu Shisheng, a Taoist master who was summoned by Genghis Khan.
  • 两朝 - Refers to the two emperors who summoned Qiu Shisheng, likely Genghis Khan and his successor, Ögedei Khan.
  • 沧海 - Refers to the vast ocean, symbolizing the petitioner's journey and the emperor's search.
  • 沙碛 - Refers to deserts, indicating the harsh conditions the petitioner endured.
  • 军国之事 - Refers to military and state affairs, which the emperor does not expect the petitioner to be involved in.

Proper Nouns

  • 青吉斯皇帝 - Genghis Khan.
  • 真人丘师省 - Qiu Shisheng.
  • 龙儿年 - The year in which the submission was made, likely a specific year in the Mongol calendar.
  • 十月哈喇 - The place where the response was received, likely a specific location in the Mongol Empire.
